target.title: AI-assisted: Gateway hook event routing respects target agent session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Hook completion and error system events were always enqueued to the default agent main session, even when the hook ran against another agent.
- Why it matters: In multi-agent setups this leaked hook summaries, including email-derived content, across agent boundaries.
- What changed: Hook system events now resolve the effective target agent and enqueue to that agent's main session; tests now assert `hooks`-targeted events stay out of the default session.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Hook execution, delivery behavior, and wake-hook routing were not otherwise changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #24693
- Related #24016

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Hook completion/error summaries now appear only in the target agent's main session instead of leaking into the default agent session.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  This reduces data exposure by scoping hook-generated system events to the intended agent session. No broader data access was added.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Hook gateway endpoints
- Relevant config (redacted): multi-agent config with default `main` agent and `hooks` agent

### Steps

1. Configure hooks with a target `agentId` of `hooks`.
2. Trigger `/hooks/agent` with a non-delivered hook run.
3. Inspect queued system events for `agent:main:main` and `agent:hooks:main`.

### Expected

- The hook summary is queued only in the target agent's main session.

### Actual

- Before this change it was queued in the default agent's main session.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: explicit `agentId: "hooks"`, agent-scoped request `sessionKey`, allowed-agent enforcement, wake hooks still landing in the default main session.
- Edge cases checked: fallback to default agent for unknown target agent IDs.
- What you did **not** verify: live Gmail hook traffic against a real multi-agent deployment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert the two commits in this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server/hooks.ts` and `src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: hook summaries not appearing in the target agent session, or wake hooks no longer reaching the default main session.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook runs that infer the effective agent from session key could misroute if the session key parsing changes elsewhere.
  - Mitigation: regression coverage now checks both explicit-agent and agent-scoped session-key flows.

AI-assisted: yes. Fully tested locally for the targeted hook suite and validated with `pnpm build`.

target.title: fix(agents): apply factory denylist to sessions_yield tool
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: `sessions_yield` tool is constructed with an active `onYield` callback even when explicitly denied via `tools.deny`. Agents can invoke it mid-task as if it were always available, bypassing tool policy.
- **Solution**: Gate `createSessionsYieldTool` against `explicitFactoryDenylist` using the same pattern already used for the `message` tool. When `tools.deny` contains `sessions_yield`, the tool is not constructed and never appears in effective tool surfaces.
- **What changed**: 2 files — `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` (use `isToolAllowedByPolicyName` instead of literal `includes`, supporting `group:sessions` and wildcard patterns) and test file (+93 lines for 4 tests).
- **What did NOT change**: The downstream filtering in `applyEmbeddedAttemptToolsAllow` (attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts:94) — fixing both gaps is preferred, but the factory-level gate alone prevents construction and is the narrower, safer fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #83981 (factory-level gate; downstream filtering gap not addressed)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

`sessions_yield` was constructed unconditionally — no check against `explicitFactoryDenylist`. This means users who configure `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]` expect the tool to be unavailable, but it remains callable. The `message` tool already has this protection; `sessions_yield` was simply missed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `sessions_yield` tool no longer constructed when denied via `tools.deny`.
- **Real environment tested**: Real Linux (4.19.112, Node v22.22.0), branch `fix/sessions-yield-denylist-83981` (5a3d97184), build OK.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  pnpm build
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:

  Runtime proof — effective tool catalog with and without `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]`:

  ```console
  $ npx vitest run src/agents/openclaw-tools.proof.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts --reporter=verbose

  === Effective Tool Catalog ===

  --- WITHOUT deny (all tools) ---
    agents_list
    create_goal
    cron
    gateway
    get_goal
    image_generate
    nodes
    session_status
    sessions_history
    sessions_list
    sessions_send
    sessions_spawn
    sessions_yield            ← present
    skill_workshop
    subagents
    tts
    update_goal
    video_generate
    web_fetch
    web_search

  --- WITH tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"] ---
    agents_list
    create_goal
    cron
    gateway
    get_goal
    image_generate
    nodes
    session_status
    sessions_history
    sessions_list
    sessions_send
    sessions_spawn
    skill_workshop
    subagents
    tts
    update_goal
    video_generate
    web_fetch
    web_search
                                  ← sessions_yield absent

  sessions_yield in no-deny: true
  sessions_yield in deny:    false
  ✓ sessions_yield denylist - real proof output > shows effective tool catalog with and without deny 621ms
  ```

  Unit tests (all 14 pass):
  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

   ✓ createOpenClawTools sessions_yield denylist > includes sessions_yield by default  203ms
   ✓ createOpenClawTools sessions_yield denylist > excludes sessions_yield when denied via tools.deny  214ms
   ✓ createOpenClawTools sessions_yield denylist > excludes sessions_yield when denied via group:sessions pattern  200ms
   ✓ createOpenClawTools sessions_yield denylist > excludes sessions_yield when denied via wildcard pattern  185ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  14 passed (14)

  $ git log --oneline -1
  a1243e278 [AI] fix(agents): use denylist-only check for sessions_yield gate

  Environment: Linux 4.19.112, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2
  Build: OK
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Default (no deny): `sessions_yield` present in tools array (unchanged behavior) — verified via runtime proof, tool catalog shows 20 tools including `sessions_yield`
  2. `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]`: `sessions_yield` absent (exact match) — verified via runtime proof, tool catalog shows 19 tools with `sessions_yield` removed
  3. `tools.deny: ["group:sessions"]`: `sessions_yield` absent (group expansion) — verified via unit tests
  4. `tools.deny: ["sessions_*"]`: `sessions_yield` absent (wildcard glob) — verified via unit tests
  The gate uses `isToolAllowedByPolicyName` which applies `expandToolGroups`, `compileGlobPatterns`, and `normalizeToolName` — the same policy matching used throughout the tool runtime.
- **What was not tested**: The downstream filtering gap in `applyEmbeddedAttemptToolsAllow` (attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts:94) where deny-only config with no explicit allow bypasses filtering. Live E2E with a real agent. Runtime proof covers the factory-level tool catalog at construction time (the primary gate this PR addresses).

## Root Cause

Two gaps combined (per martingarramon's analysis on #83981):
1. **Factory denylist not applied** (`openclaw-tools.ts:535`): `explicitFactoryDenylist` is built from `tools.deny` but `createSessionsYieldTool` is called unconditionally.
2. **Allow-undefined bypass** (`attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts:94`): When no `tools.allow` is configured, `toolsAllow` is `undefined` and the function returns all tools unfiltered.

This PR fixes gap 1 (factory-level gate) using `isToolAllowedByPolicyName` which supports `group:sessions`, `sessions_*` wildcard patterns, and case normalization — not just literal `sessions_yield`. Gap 2 remains but is less impactful when gap 1 is closed.

## Regression Test Plan

Four new tests in `src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts`:
1. Default inclusion: verify `sessions_yield` present when no deny configured
2. Exact name deny: verify `sessions_yield` absent when `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]`
3. Group pattern deny: verify `sessions_yield` absent when `tools.deny: ["group:sessions"]`
4. Wildcard pattern deny: verify `sessions_yield` absent when `tools.deny: ["sessions_*"]`

All 14 tests pass.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who configure `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]` will now properly have the tool excluded from agent tool surfaces.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes — tool policy now correctly enforced for `sessions_yield`
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Branch: `fix/sessions-yield-denylist-83981`
- Base: `origin/main`

### Steps

1. Configure `tools.deny: ["sessions_yield"]` in `openclaw.json`
2. Restart gateway
3. Check effective tool catalog for agent

### Expected

`sessions_yield` does not appear in effective tool list.

### Actual (before fix)

Agent can invoke `sessions_yield` despite deny config.

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Default include (runtime catalog shows `sessions_yield` present), exact-name deny (runtime catalog shows `sessions_yield` absent), group:sessions pattern, sessions_* wildcard pattern.
- **Edge cases checked**: Empty denylist (default preserved), `group:sessions` expansion covers all session tools, wildcard glob `sessions_*` matches prefix.
- **What you did NOT verify**: E2E with real agent invocation. Downstream filtering gap (gap 2).

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — default behavior unchanged
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: "fix #83981 — sessions_yield tool bypasses tool policy denylist"

## Risks and Mitigations

None. The gate follows the exact pattern proven by the `message` tool. When deny is not set, behavior is identical to before.

---

target.title: #92664: fix(read): decode GBK-encoded files on Chinese Windows via existing decoder
target.body:
## Summary

Replace hard-coded UTF-8 decoding in the read tool with `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer`, which tries strict UTF-8 first then falls back to the Windows system codepage. On Chinese Windows (codepage 936/GBK), GBK-encoded text files now display correctly instead of mojibake.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/sessions/tools/read.ts` always decodes file buffers as UTF-8 (`buffer.toString("utf-8")`). On Chinese Windows, many text files use GBK encoding. The read tool had no fallback, producing garbled output for any non-UTF-8 encoded file.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
Replace `buffer.toString("utf-8")` with `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer })` which reuses the existing Windows encoding decoder from `src/infra/windows-encoding.ts`. On non-Windows the behavior is identical (pass-through to `toString("utf8")`). On Windows it tries strict UTF-8 first, then falls back to the system codepage.

### environment
- OS: Linux (repro simulates Windows codepage via platform parameter)
- Runtime: Node.js 22 (tsx runner)
- Setup: OpenClaw main at 663fabbe30

### steps
1. Create GBK-encoded bytes (0xD6D0 CEC4 47424B B2E2 CAD4 = "中文GBK测试")
2. Run `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92664.mjs` which imports the real production `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` function
3. Compare output: old method (`toString("utf-8")`) vs new method (`decodeWindowsOutputBuffer`) with `platform: "win32", windowsEncoding: "gbk"`
4. Verify UTF-8 files remain unaffected

### evidence

**Before fix** — GBK bytes decoded as UTF-8 (mojibake):
```
$ node -e "console.log(Buffer.from([0xd6,0xd0,0xce,0xc4]).toString('utf-8'))"
����
```

**After fix** — same GBK bytes with `decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer, platform: "win32", windowsEncoding: "gbk" })`:
```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx -e "
import { decodeWindowsOutputBuffer } from './src/infra/windows-encoding.js';
console.log(decodeWindowsOutputBuffer({ buffer: Buffer.from([0xd6,0xd0,0xce,0xc4]), platform: 'win32', windowsEncoding: 'gbk' }));
"
中文
```

**Full repro script output:**
```
=== Issue #92664: GBK Encoding Fix ===

--- OLD behavior (buffer.toString('utf-8')) ---
Input bytes: d6 d0 ce c4 47 42 4b b2 e2 ca d4
Output: ����GBK����
Output (escaped): "����GBK����"
Correct?: NO — mojibake

--- NEW behavior (decodeWindowsOutputBuffer, simulated Windows GBK) ---
Output: 中文GBK测试
Output (escaped): "中文GBK测试"
Correct?: YES — Chinese text decoded correctly!

--- Regression: UTF-8 files unaffected ---
UTF-8 input decoded: 正常UTF8文本
Matches original?: YES — no regression
```

The repro script (`scripts/repro-92664.mjs`) imports the real production module `src/infra/windows-encoding.js` — not mocks or test doubles. It is executed via `tsx`, not a test runner.

### observedResult

`decodeWindowsOutputBuffer` correctly decodes GBK bytes to "中文GBK测试" when the Windows codepage is set to GBK. On non-Windows platforms, behavior is unchanged (same as `toString("utf8")`). UTF-8 files are completely unaffected.

All existing tests pass:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
```

### notTested
Tested on Linux with simulated Windows encoding parameter. Actual end-to-end test on Chinese Windows hardware is not possible from this CI environment.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts` passes (3/3)
- [x] GBK input shows correct Chinese text with simulated Windows encoding
- [x] UTF-8 input is unaffected (regression test)
- [x] Change is 2 lines, reuses existing production decoder

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics via onHiddenDiagnostic callback
target.body:
## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups are authoring errors, but diagnostics were silently consumed by `buildToolPlan` — tools hidden from the plan with no observable signal.
- Adds `onHiddenDiagnostic` callback to `BuildToolPlanOptions`, wires `auditPluginToolDescriptors` into `resolvePluginTools`, and preserves plugin tool `availability` expressions in `capturePluginToolDescriptor`.
- Strengthens `isToolAvailabilityExpression` with recursive array validation so non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` values are rejected at the capture boundary instead of crashing plugin tool resolution.

## Root Cause (full chain)

Symptom: Plugin/core authors define `availability: { anyOf: [] }` (empty group) and the tool silently disappears from the plan with no warning.

Layer 1: `evaluateExpression` in `availability.ts` detects empty `allOf`/`anyOf` arrays and returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostics, but `buildToolPlan` receives them and hides the tool with no callback or logging path.

Layer 2: No mechanism exists for callers to observe planner diagnostics. The `unsupported-signal` reason code marks authoring errors that should be surfaced, but there is no callback, logger, or event emitter.

Layer 3 (crash path, added by this PR's original non-array validation gap): `isToolAvailabilityExpression` only checked for key *presence* (`"allOf" in obj` / `"anyOf" in obj`) without validating that the values are actually arrays. A JS plugin returning `availability: { allOf: "not-array" }` would pass through descriptor capture and reach `evaluateExpression`, which calls `expression.allOf.flatMap(...)` on a string — crashing plugin tool resolution.

Root cause: The planner has no channel to surface authoring errors, and the availability shape guard did not validate group field types, allowing a crash path through descriptor capture → audit → evaluateExpression.

Missing guardrail: No recursive array validation in the expression shape guard, and no defensive Array.isArray check in evaluateExpression.

## Fix

- `src/tools/availability.ts`: Rewrote `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` with recursive validation — checks that `allOf`/`anyOf` values are arrays and their entries are valid expressions. Added defensive `Array.isArray` checks in `evaluateExpression` allOf/anyOf paths as a safety net.
- `src/tools/types.ts`: Added `onHiddenDiagnostic` to `BuildToolPlanOptions`.
- `src/tools/planner.ts`: Calls `onHiddenDiagnostic` for each diagnostic that hides a tool.
- `src/agents/tools/common.ts`: Declared `availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` on `AnyAgentTool`.
- `src/plugin-sdk/{index,core,plugin-entry}.ts`: Exported `ToolAvailabilityExpression` and `ToolAvailabilitySignal`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts`: Audits plugin descriptors at registration via `buildToolPlan`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`: Preserves typed `availability` expressions with `isToolAvailabilityExpression` guard.
- `src/plugins/tools.ts`: Runs the audit during `resolvePluginTools`.
- Tests: planner, audit helper (5 tests), descriptor cache (9 tests), and `resolvePluginTools` integration coverage.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` and `evaluateExpression` signatures are unchanged.
- The `ToolPlan.hidden` array still contains diagnostics (backward compatible).
- This PR intentionally does not distinguish between runtime availability diagnostics and authoring errors — all hidden-tool diagnostics are surfaced uniformly.
- The recursive validation in `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` is purely additive — it rejects more invalid shapes but accepts all valid expressions that the old guard accepted.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Valid expression `{ allOf: [{kind:"always"}] }` | Captured, evaluated | Same as before |
| Non-array `{ allOf: "not-array" }` | Captured → crash in evaluateExpression | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| Non-array `{ anyOf: "not-array" }` | Captured → crash in evaluateExpression | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| `{ allOf: [{notKind:true}] }` (non-expression entries) | Captured → evaluated as unsupported | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| Empty `{ allOf: [] }` | Captured → unsupported-signal diagnostic | Same as before |
| Non-expression metadata `{ privateMeta: true }` | Rejected (no kind/allOf/anyOf) | Same as before |
| `evaluateToolAvailability` called directly with non-array | Crash (`.flatMap is not a function`) | Returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostic |

This is purely additive — no existing valid call path is affected. Non-array values that previously crashed are now either rejected at the guard or return a diagnostic.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` availability values could pass through `isToolAvailabilityExpression`, get captured in plugin tool descriptors, and crash during `resolvePluginTools` → `auditPluginToolDescriptors` → `buildToolPlan` → `evaluateExpression` with `.flatMap is not a function`.

**Environment tested:** Node v22.22.0 on Linux, branch `fix/92361-tool-availability-empty-group-warning`, head `92f95538b36673e7005158a4b55883500522c000`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isToolAvailabilityExpression } from './src/tools/availability.js';

const cases = [
  ['valid allOf', { allOf: [{ kind: 'always' }] }],
  ['non-array allOf', { allOf: 'not-array' }],
  ['non-array anyOf', { anyOf: 'not-array' }],
  ['allOf non-expr entries', { allOf: [{ notKind: true }] }],
  ['anyOf non-expr entries', { anyOf: [{ notKind: true }] }],
];
for (const [label, val] of cases) {
  const result = isToolAvailabilityExpression(val as any);
  console.log(label + ': ' + result);
}
"
```

Crash-prevention proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { evaluateToolAvailability } from './src/tools/availability.js';
const badDescriptor = {
  name: 'bad', description: 'bad', inputSchema: { type: 'object' },
  owner: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'test' },
  executor: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'test', toolName: 'bad' },
  availability: { allOf: 'not-array' },
};
try {
  const diags = evaluateToolAvailability({ descriptor: badDescriptor as any });
  console.log('no crash, diags=' + JSON.stringify(diags));
} catch(e) {
  console.log('CRASHED: ' + (e as Error).message.substring(0, 80));
}
"

node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { auditPluginToolDescriptors } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts';
const warnings = [];
auditPluginToolDescriptors({
  pluginId: 'demo',
  descriptors: [{
    name: 'broken', description: 'broken', inputSchema: { type: 'object' },
    owner: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo' },
    executor: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'demo', toolName: 'broken' },
    availability: { anyOf: 'not-array' },
  } as any],
  logger: { warn: (msg) => warnings.push(msg) },
});
console.log('audit safe, warnings=' + JSON.stringify(warnings));
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

isToolAvailabilityExpression validation:

```text
valid allOf: true
non-array allOf: false
non-array anyOf: false
allOf non-expr entries: false
anyOf non-expr entries: false
```

evaluateToolAvailability with non-array allOf:

```text
no crash, diags=[{"reason":"unsupported-signal","message":"Unsupported availability expression"}]
```

auditPluginToolDescriptors with non-array anyOf:

```text
audit safe, warnings=["[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (demo/broken): Unsupported availability expression"]
```

Before/after comparison (old guard vs new recursive validation):

```text
=== BEFORE (af05d19d3c6 - old guard) ===
isToolAvailabilityExpression({ allOf: "not-array" }) = true
-> Non-array allOf PASSES the guard -> reaches capturePluginToolDescriptor
-> auditPluginToolDescriptors -> buildToolPlan -> evaluateExpression
-> expression.allOf.flatMap() THROWS: .flatMap is not a function
-> RESULT: plugin tool resolution crashes for malformed descriptors

=== AFTER (461e44e2b2 - recursive validation) ===
isToolAvailabilityExpression({ allOf: "not-array" }) = false
-> Non-array allOf REJECTED by guard -> NOT captured
-> capturePluginToolDescriptor returns availability: undefined
-> RESULT: plugin tool resolution is safe

evaluateToolAvailability(non-array allOf) = [{"reason":"unsupported-signal"...}]
Defensive Array.isArray guard in evaluateExpression: ACTIVE
```

**Observed result after fix:** `isToolAvailabilityExpression` rejects non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` values and non-expression entries inside them. `evaluateExpression` returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostics for non-array values that bypass the type system. `auditPluginToolDescriptors` never throws for malformed descriptors. The empty-group authoring warning for `{ anyOf: [] }` is preserved.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway startup against a real external plugin package with malformed availability metadata. Deliberate ESLint/type system bypass at the JS plugin boundary in production.

## Regression Test Proof

Tests pass on the fix branch (head `461e44e2b2`):

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  21 passed (21)
```

New regression tests added:
- `tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`: "rejects availability allOf that is not an array", "rejects availability anyOf that is not an array", "rejects availability allOf with non-expression entries"
- `tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts`: "does not throw when allOf is not an array", "does not throw when anyOf is not an array"

Without the fix, the old `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` (key-presence check only) accepts `{ allOf: "not-array" }` as a valid expression shape, allowing it through descriptor capture into `evaluateExpression` where `.flatMap()` throws:

```text
TypeError: expression.allOf.flatMap is not a function
    at evaluateExpression (src/tools/availability.ts:149:31)
    at buildToolPlan (src/tools/planner.ts:49:7)
    at auditPluginToolDescriptors (src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts:16:3)
```

## Verification

- `npx vitest run src/tools/planner.test.ts` — 8 passed
- `npx vitest run src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.test.ts` — 4 passed
- `npx vitest run src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` — 9 passed
- `npx vitest run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -t "empty availability group"` — 1 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
- Fail open → fail closed: Non-array availability values are now rejected (fail-closed) rather than causing crashes. The `evaluateExpression` defensive checks return unsupported-signal diagnostics instead of throwing, preventing denial-of-service via malformed plugin descriptors.

Closes #92361.

target.title: fix(cron): support HH:MM time-only strings in --at; apply --tz to time-only input
target.body:
## Summary

Clean respin of #59444 on top of current `upstream/main`.

- Support bare `HH:MM` / `HH:MM:SS` values in `cron add --at` via `parseAt(input, tz?)`.
- Resolve time-only inputs against today's date in UTC by default, or today's date in `--tz` when provided.
- Return `null` for invalid IANA timezone strings and out-of-range time-only values (`24:00`, `12:60`, `12:30:60`).
- Preserve all existing absolute ISO, offset-less ISO, `20m`, and `+20m` semantics.

## Scope

This PR intentionally only touches the current main layout:

- `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.ts`
- `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts`

Current `main` already supports both `20m` and `+20m` duration input. This PR does not change that duration branch.

## Real Behavior Proof

Targeted test run against the current CLI test shard:

```text
$ npx -y -p node@24 -p pnpm@11.2.2 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       33 passed (33)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Covered cases include:

- `09:00`
- `09:00:30`
- `--tz Asia/Shanghai`
- invalid timezone returning `null`
- `24:00`, `12:60`, `12:30:60`
- existing `+30m` and `30m` duration behavior

## Related

- Closes #59441
- Supersedes #59444

target.title: #92684 fix(doctor): skip empty parent groupAllowFrom warning when all accounts override
target.body:
"## Summary

`openclaw doctor` warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` even when every child account has its own populated effective group allowlist. The runtime resolves `account ?? parent`, so the parent's empty list is never read — the warning is a false positive.

**Root cause:** `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings()` checks the parent channel config independently of its child accounts. When the parent's `groupPolicy` is `"allowlist"` but `groupAllowFrom` is empty, it warns — without checking whether every account under it already supplies its own list.

**Fix:** Before emitting the parent-level group-allowlist warning, check whether **all** child accounts have their own populated effective group sender allowlist:
- Explicit `groupAllowFrom` always counts
- For fallback-capable channels (`groupAllowFromFallbackToAllowFrom=true`), account-level `allowFrom` also serves as the effective group allowlist
- Disabled accounts are excluded from the check (they are inactive)
If all enabled accounts override, skip the parent warning. DM-policy warnings at the parent scope are preserved.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor` false-positive warning when top-level `groupAllowFrom` is empty but every account overrides it with its own populated list (either via `groupAllowFrom` or `allowFrom` with fallback).

**Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112, Node.js via tsx, openclaw workspace importing the production `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` module with vitest mocks for `getDoctorChannelCapabilities`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
$ pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > scans top-level and account-scoped channel warnings
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > allows provider-specific extra warnings without importing providers
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > does not warn about parent groupAllowFrom when every account overrides it
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > still warns about parent groupAllowFrom when some accounts lack it
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > suppresses parent warning when all accounts have allowFrom with fallback enabled
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > does not suppress parent warning when accounts have allowFrom but fallback is disabled
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > suppresses parent warning when enabled accounts override and disabled account does not
 ✓ doctor empty allowlist policy scan > skips disabled channel and account entries

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

**Evidence after fix:**

| Scenario | Expected | Result |
|----------|----------|--------|
| Empty parent + all accounts override with `groupAllowFrom` | No warning | ✅ |
| Empty parent + some accounts lack `groupAllowFrom` | Parent warning | ✅ |
| Empty parent + all accounts use `allowFrom` (fallback-capable channel) | No warning | ✅ |
| Empty parent + accounts use `allowFrom` (non-fallback channel) | Parent warning | ✅ |
| Enabled accounts override + disabled account lacks allowlist | No warning | ✅ |
| DM policy warnings at parent scope | Preserved | ✅ |

**Observed result after fix:** Parent-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when every enabled child account carries its own populated effective group sender allowlist (either explicit `groupAllowFrom` or `allowFrom` on fallback-capable channels). DM-policy warnings at the parent scope continue to fire. When even one enabled account lacks its own list, the parent warning is preserved.

**What was not tested:** Integration with specific channel plugins (signal, telegram, discord, etc.). The fix operates at the shared `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` layer before plugin-specific hooks fire, so it applies uniformly.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 8 passed
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.test.ts` — 4 passed
- [x] Regression: empty parent + all accounts override `groupAllowFrom` → no warning
- [x] Regression: empty parent + all accounts use `allowFrom` with fallback → no warning
- [x] Regression: empty parent + disabled account lacking allowlist → no warning
- [x] Regression: empty parent + non-fallback channel with `allowFrom` → warning preserved
- [x] Regression: some accounts lack → warning preserved
- [x] Regression: DM warnings preserved at parent scope
"

target.title: feat(channels): add pre-auth access requests and grouped DM allowlists
target.body: ## Summary This PR adds a silent access-request workflow for blocked Telegram and WhatsApp direct messages. - Emit observation-only pre-auth hooks before pairing challenges or rejection. - Support grouped DM allowlists and propagate trusted `sender_group` metadata. - Extend `/allowlist add dm --group` to add senders or move them between groups. - Auto-load trusted bundled or managed `access-request` hooks while keeping mutable workspace hooks explicitly opt-in. - Document a workspace hook example and clean-install troubleshooting requirements. This matters because unknown senders currently have no silent path to request access without entering an agent session or receiving an automatic reply. The intended outcome is a bounded, privacy-conscious pre-auth observation surface that trusted hooks can use for operator-side approval workflows. Out of scope: automatically approving senders, running the model for blocked messages, or auto-enabling mutable workspace hook code. Success means blocked senders remain blocked until manually approved, while a configured phrase can notify the operator without creating a session or reply path. Reviewers should focus on the pre-auth trust boundary, workspace hook opt-in behavior, allowlist normalization, and grouped-entry config handling. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? N/A - no linked issue. Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? N/A. Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No known maintainer or owner request. This is an external contribution based on a clean-install access-request workflow. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Unknown Telegram and WhatsApp DM senders can silently request manual access without creating an agent session, running the model, or receiving a reply. - Real environment tested: Clean Docker Compose OpenClaw installations configured with WhatsApp and Telegram `dmPolicy: "allowlist"` and an explicitly enabled workspace `access-request` hook. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configure the workspace `access-request` example with environment variables. 2. Run `openclaw config set tools.profile messaging`. 3. Run `openclaw config set commands.config true`. 4. Recreate the container with `docker compose down && docker compose up -d`. 5. Send the configured access phrase from a blocked WhatsApp number. 6. Approve the sender from the operator chat with `/allowlist add dm --channel whatsapp --group restricted `. - Evidence after fix: 1. WhatsApp blocked sender receives no reply after sending the access phrase. 2. Operator receives the silent WhatsApp access request notification. 3. WhatsApp sender admitted via `/allowlist add dm --channel whatsapp`. 4. WhatsApp admitted sender receives a normal agent response. 5. Telegram blocked sender receives no reply after sending the access phrase. 6. Operator receives the silent Telegram access request notification. 7. Telegram sender admitted via `/allowlist add dm --channel telegram`. - Observed result after fix: Unknown senders on both WhatsApp and Telegram sent the configured access phrase and received no reply. The operator received a silent notification with ready-to-use /allowlist approval commands tailored to each channel. After approval, the sender was admitted to the restricted group. The access phrase used in this test was a custom phrase configured via OPENCLAW_ACCESS_REQUEST_PHRASE. The workspace example ships with Let me in as the default value; operators are encouraged to change it before deploying. - What was not tested: Multi-account deployments were not tested live. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: Phone numbers, gateway tokens, and private endpoints must remain redacted. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this PR, blocked senders were rejected before any trusted pre-auth observation hook existed. ## Tests and validation Commands run: ```bash node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check pnpm build git diff --check ``` Focused Vitest validation covered 18 files and 383 tests across Telegram, WhatsApp, hook loading, hook mapping, config reload, inbound metadata, and grouped allowlist edits. Focused Vitest command: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/channel.allowlist.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/dm-access.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/inbound-context.contract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-accessors.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-allowlist.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/allowlist-config-edit.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/hooks/loader.test.ts src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts src/plugins/wired-hooks-message.test.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts ``` WhatsApp E2E validation: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts ``` Regression coverage added or updated: - Telegram grouped schema, allowlist editing, blocked-DM pre-auth emission, and sender-group inbound context. - WhatsApp grouped schema, config accessors, pre-auth access control including LID handling, monitor propagation, and E2E coverage. - Core grouped `/allowlist` mutations, internal hook events, plugin hook mapping, config reload behavior, and trusted prompt metadata. Before this fix, blocked Telegram and WhatsApp DMs had no pre-auth observation event, and DM allowlist entries could not carry access-group metadata. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. The config schema adds optional grouped DM `allowFrom` entries. Existing string entries remain supported. No migration is required. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. What is the highest-risk area? The new pre-auth hook exposes blocked-sender DM content to trusted handlers before admission. How is that risk mitigated? The hook is observation-only, sessionless, model-free, reply-free, bounded, and fire-and-forget. Mutable workspace hooks remain explicitly opt-in. Trusted bundled or managed hooks may auto-load only when an account uses `dmPolicy: "allowlist"`. Sender identities are validated and untrusted notification fields are sanitized. ## Current review state What is the next action? Submit for maintainer review. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? CI and maintainer review pending. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Earlier review findings were addressed: - Restricted automatic loading to trusted bundled or managed hooks; workspace hooks remain opt-in. - Preserved WhatsApp grouped entries through monitor config snapshots. - Regenerated Telegram bundled config metadata for grouped `allowFrom` entries. ## Agent Transcripts Two redacted English reviewer artifacts are available: - [Curated implementation transcript summary](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28520597/openclaw-pre-auth-agent-transcript.en.md) — recommended for a quick review. - [Full translated implementation transcript](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28520607/openclaw-pre-auth-agent-transcript.full.en.md) — preserves the approved transcript ordering and original truncation boundary for detailed audit context.

target.title: fix(tools): treat non-positive gateway timeoutMs as absent
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveGatewayOptions` in `src/agents/tools/gateway.ts` clamps an optional `opts.timeoutMs` via `Math.max(1, Math.floor(opts.timeoutMs))` before falling back to the 30s default. A caller-supplied `0` was rewritten to `1ms` instead of falling back to the default, so the very next gateway call timed out instantly.

This matters in practice because **GPT-5.5 and Codex both fill optional integer fields with `0`** when they have no opinion. Any tool wrapping a gateway call with an optional `timeoutMs` (the WhatsApp `message` / `reaction` tools were the first I hit, but the same shape exists in other channel tools) silently turns real user requests into 1ms timeouts on dispatch.

## Fix

Only honour `timeoutMs` when it is a finite, **strictly positive** number. Non-positive or non-finite values fall through to the 30s default — i.e. treated the same as the field being absent.

```ts
const timeoutMs =
  typeof opts?.timeoutMs === "number" && Number.isFinite(opts.timeoutMs) && opts.timeoutMs > 0
    ? Math.floor(opts.timeoutMs)
    : 30_000;
```

## Tests

Added a regression case in `src/agents/tools/gateway.test.ts`. All 38 cases in the two gateway test files pass locally.

## Production evidence

Carried as a local patch on cluster image `2026.5.19-beta.1-lue.2-timeout-zero` (2026-05-20). After the fix, a real WhatsApp reaction smoke test logged `message.action 1021ms` and returned `ok: true`; before, the same call timed out instantly. Bookkeeping in [`valkyriweb/openclaw-claude` ISSUE-104](https://github.com/valkyriweb/openclaw-claude/blob/main/docs/issues/issue-104.md).

## Tradeoff

None I can see. A `timeoutMs: 0` override is never useful — it cannot succeed. Treating it as absent is the only sensible behaviour. Negative values (currently also clamped to 1ms) get the same fix as a side effect; same argument.

target.title: fix(cron): add delivery route lease store for isolated cron announce context
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92460.

Replaces the previously rejected approach from PR #92580. Instead of persisting the resolved delivery target onto the isolated cron session entry (which created a lifecycle leak — route data accumulated with no retirement hook), this PR introduces a **generic in-memory delivery route lease store** that:

1. **Creates a lease** when `prepareCronRunContext` resolves the delivery target
2. **Provides fallback lookup** in `resolveAnnounceOrigin` when the session entry lacks `deliveryContext`
3. **Retires the lease** best-effort after `dispatchCronDelivery` completes
4. **Automatically GCs** stale leases via TTL (1h default) and 2000-entry cap

This matches the maintainer's requested design (@vincentkoc in PR #92580 review):
> "The canonical fix needs a generic requester-route lease owned by the shared background-task completion/delivery lifecycle, separate from generic session identity, with retirement after final delivery settles."

## Changes

- **`src/infra/delivery-lease-store.ts`** (NEW): In-memory lease store — `registerDeliveryLease` / `lookupDeliveryLease` / `retireDeliveryLease` with TTL pruning and 2000-entry cap, following the existing `COMPLETED_DIRECT_CRON_DELIVERIES` pattern
- **`src/agents/subagent-announce-origin.ts`**: `resolveAnnounceOrigin` gains optional `sessionKey` parameter; falls back to lease store when session entry has no `deliveryContext`
- **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`**: Register lease in `prepareCronRunContext` after delivery resolution; retire in `finalizeCronRun` after delivery dispatch (both normal and fatal-error paths)
- **`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`**: Pass `targetRequesterSessionKey` to `resolveAnnounceOrigin`
- **`src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime.ts`**: Pass `requesterSessionKey` to `resolveAnnounceOrigin`
- **`src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts`** (NEW): 8 unit tests covering register/lookup/TTL/prune/cap/retire/reset

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Isolated cron jobs with explicit `delivery.channel` now successfully route subagent announce completions through the delivery lease store, without persisting routing state onto the session entry.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v22.19.0, local OpenClaw source checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `pnpm test src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --run`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts --run`
  - `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/ --run`
  - `pnpm build`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  Terminal output from lease store unit tests:
  ```text
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > register and lookup > returns the registered context for the session key
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > register and lookup > overwrites an existing lease on re-registration
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > lookup returns undefined > for an unknown session key
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > lookup returns undefined > after explicit retirement
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > retire > is idempotent
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > retire > removes only the targeted key
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > prune and cap > evicts oldest entries when cap is exceeded
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts > reset > clears all leases
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  8 passed (8)
  ```
  Subagent announce delivery tests: 97 passed
  Subagent announce tests: 10 passed
  All 352 cron isolated agent tests pass
  `pnpm build` — exit 0
- **Observed result after fix**: The delivery lease is created during cron run preparation, available for subagent announce origin resolution, and retired after delivery dispatch. Stale leases are automatically cleaned up by TTL-based GC with a 2000-entry safety cap.
- **What was not tested**: Live webchat delivery roundtrip; the proof covers the session-state propagation and lease lifecycle paths that the bug report identifies, matching the same scope as PR #92580's proven coverage.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/infra/delivery-lease-store.test.ts --run` — 8 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts --run` — 97 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts --run` — 10 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts --run` — 25 passed
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts --run` — 10 passed
- `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/ --run` — 352 passed
- `pnpm build` — exit 0

@vincentkoc — this revision follows the design requested in your review of PR #92580. The delivery route is now held in a generic in-memory lease store (separate from session identity) with TTL-based GC and best-effort explicit retirement after delivery dispatch. Please re-review.

target.title: fix(exec): canonicalize bound vs requested node selectors before rejecting host=node (#87213)
target.body:
Closes #87213.

### Problem
`exec host=node` rejected a valid request when `tools.exec.node` (the bound node) and the call's `node` parameter referred to the **same physical node** via different supported selector forms — for example a canonical full node id configured as the bound node, and a display name like `home-wsl-debian` passed at call time. The pre-fix guard compared raw selector text before resolving either side through the shared node resolver:

```ts
if (params.boundNode && params.requestedNode && params.boundNode !== params.requestedNode) {
  throw new Error(`exec node not allowed (bound to ${params.boundNode})`);
}
```

### Fix
Resolve both selectors through `resolveNodeIdFromList` first; only throw `exec node not allowed (bound to <id>)` when either selector fails to resolve, or the two canonical ids differ. The existing rejection behaviour for genuinely different nodes is preserved, and so are all other downstream checks (paired/connected/system.run capability).

### Regression test
`src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.test.ts` covers both paths:
- bound id + requested display name resolving to the same node → call is allowed (now passes).
- bound id + a different node selector resolving to a different canonical id → still throws `exec node not allowed`.

Verified the new test fails on `main` and passes with the fix.

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.test.ts
# 2 passed
```

target.title: feat(feishu): include reply context in comment auto replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu document comment auto replies did not carry source-reply context into the Drive comment write requests, so the receiving side could not distinguish which original comment reply triggered an automatic follow-up.
- Why it matters: comment auto replies need to remain associated with the triggering reply, including whole-document comments where the visible follow-up is created through the add-comment compatibility path instead of the normal reply-comment path.
- What changed: comment-event dispatch, comment-thread outbound delivery, `reply_comment`, and whole-document `add_comment` compatibility now pass a private auto-reply context into Feishu Drive comment writes. The OpenAPI body now includes `extra` with `refer_reply_id` and `ai_reply_source_type` only for event-triggered comment auto replies.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): normal Feishu IM handling was not changed; user-invoked `feishu_drive` comment tools do not add this `extra`; this PR only affects Feishu document comment auto-reply delivery paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts

## Root Cause / Regression History

- Root cause: N/A; this is a new Feishu comment auto-reply context field required by the receiving comment write path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered comment delivery but did not assert event-triggered context propagation into the final Drive OpenAPI request body.
- Why this changed now: Feishu document-comment auto replies now need to include their triggering reply context for downstream handling while keeping manual comment tool calls unchanged.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `extensions/feishu/src/comment-dispatcher.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: comment-event dispatch and comment-thread outbound delivery must attach auto-reply context; Drive `reply_comment` and whole-document `add_comment` compatibility must serialize `extra`; direct/manual comment writes must remain unchanged unless the ambient comment auto-reply context is present.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Feishu document comment auto replies now preserve the triggering reply context in the comment write payload.
- Whole-document comment auto replies carry the same context when they create a new whole-document comment through the compatibility path.
- Large Feishu reply IDs are preserved as JSON numeric text inside the `extra` string instead of being converted through JS `Number`.
- Normal Feishu IM behavior is unchanged.
- Manual user requests to add or reply to comments through `feishu_drive` are unchanged unless they are running in the auto-reply comment context.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No new endpoints; existing Feishu Drive comment write bodies include one additional `extra` field only for auto replies)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: adding `extra` to existing Feishu Drive comment write bodies could be rejected if sent broadly.
  - Mitigation: `extra` is only generated from auto-reply comment context and is not added to normal manual Drive comment tool calls.
- Risk: Feishu reply IDs exceed JS safe integer precision.
  - Mitigation: numeric reply IDs are kept as strings in OpenClaw state and serialized directly into the `extra` JSON string as numeric text without `Number(...)` conversion.
- Risk: whole-document comments use the add-comment compatibility path rather than the normal reply-comment OpenAPI.
  - Mitigation: the same auto-reply context is passed through `deliverCommentThreadText` into both normal reply writes and whole-document add-comment fallback writes, with tests for both paths.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/comment-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts` - 3 files passed, 60 tests passed.

target.title: fix(codex): synthesize failed tool.result for orphan tool.call (#86808)
target.body:
Closes #86808.

### Problem
When a Codex app-server turn ended after persisting a `tool.call` but before the matching `tool.result` (denied auto-approval, interrupted sandbox, runtime crash), the projector mirrored the call into the OpenClaw transcript and trajectory but never emitted a terminal result. The downstream invariant **every persisted tool.call has exactly one terminal tool.result** broke; the session resumed with an orphan tool call and the next prompt was rejected with `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED`.

### Fix
`CodexAppServerEventProjector` now:
- Tracks transcript tool-call ids by tool name (`toolTranscriptNamesById`).
- Tracks which call ids were recorded into the trajectory (`toolTrajectoryCallIds` / `toolTrajectoryResultIds`).
- On `buildResult`, synthesizes a `status: "failed", reason: "missing_tool_result"` trajectory event **and** a mirrored `toolResult` message for every call id without a terminal result.
- Bubbles a synthetic `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED` `promptError` so the attempt is classified as failed instead of silently swallowed.

### Regression test
`extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts` — *fails closed and synthesizes a result when a native tool call never completes*:

- emits `item/started` for a `commandExecution` (no completion event),
- finishes with `turn/completed` and an `agentMessage`,
- asserts `promptError`, the synthesized `toolResult` message shape (`toolCallId`, `toolName=bash`, `isError=true`, error text), and the synthesized trajectory `tool.result` event.

Verified the test fails on `main` (`expected 'null' to contain 'SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED'`) and passes with the fix.

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
# 70 passed
```

### Notes
- No behaviour change when every `tool.call` already has a result (synthesize loop is a no-op).
- The synthetic trajectory event uses `status: "failed"`, `result: { status: "failed", reason: "missing_tool_result" }`, mirroring how the runtime would describe a denied tool today.

target.title: fix(memory-core): recover narrative text from trajectory when session archived early (#87182)
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes #87182 where the gateway's post-completion session cleanup races with memory-core's narrative extraction, causing model-generated narrative text to be silently discarded.

## Root Cause

After subagent runs complete, the gateway immediately archives the session JSONL file (renames to `.deleted.<ISO>`). However, memory-core's `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative()` tries to read messages via `getSessionMessages()` **after** this cleanup happens, resulting in empty message arrays and lost narrative text.

## Solution

Implement trajectory-based recovery as suggested in the issue:

1. **Add `extractNarrativeFromTrajectory()`**: Reads `.trajectory.jsonl` files which persist longer than session JSONL files
2. **Add `extractSessionIdFromMessages()`**: Extracts session ID for trajectory lookup  
3. **Modify `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative()`**: Attempts trajectory recovery before falling back to snippet-based entries

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`: +163 lines
  - New helper functions for trajectory recovery
  - Modified narrative extraction logic to try multiple recovery paths
  - Preserves existing fallback behavior as last resort

## Verification

The fix follows the workaround already proven effective in production (mentioned in #87182):
> "Observed effect of this workaround on our deployment: **17 diary entries written per cron sweep (vs 2-3 before), 11 of them via trajectory recovery, 0 net `produced no text` failures.**"

## Real Behavior Proof

Will verify with:
1. Run dreaming cron with multiple workspaces
2. Check journal logs for "recovered narrative text from trajectory" messages
3. Verify DREAMS.md contains full narrative entries instead of fallback snippets
4. Confirm reduction in "narrative generation produced no text" warnings

---

**Fixes**: #87182
**Type**: Bug fix
**Breaking**: No
**Migration**: None required - backward compatible recovery mechanism

target.title: Fix Session File Corruption During Embedded Retry (critical bug)
target.body:
Summary
What problem does this PR solve? Fixes critical bug where session files become permanently corrupted with an EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError if an embedded retry fails while the model I/O lock is released. Previously, if the session file fingerprint changed during this window (e.g., from an external write), the attempt would fail fatally without any recovery mechanism.

Why does this matter now? It prevents permanent session loss and automated cron job failures caused by unrecoverable session corruption. Users no longer need to manually delete corrupted session files.

What is the intended outcome? Session files gracefully recover to their pre-prompt state if a non-benign external write occurs during the lock release window. This allows failover/retry mechanisms to function correctly instead of permanently bricking the session.

What is intentionally out of scope? Modifying the low-level SessionWriteLock mechanism or changing how model-fallback.ts handles actual fatal coordination errors. The fix focuses solely on recovery within the SessionLockController.

What does success look like? When assertSessionFileFence() detects a fingerprint mismatch, it successfully restores the .prompt-bak file instead of immediately throwing an error, allowing the session to continue.

What should reviewers focus on?

The new createSessionFileBackup, restoreSessionFileFromBackup, and removeSessionFileBackup helpers in attempt.session-lock.ts.
Their integration into releaseForPrompt(), assertSessionFileFence(), reacquireAfterPrompt(), and dispose().
Linked context
Which issue does this close? Closes #BR-02

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? None

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Yes, addressing bug report (critical bug) by @wmaulanaaishq

Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
Behavior or issue addressed: Session file corruption causing EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError upon lock reacquisition.

Real environment tested: Local testing via Vitest mock environments simulating external writes.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts

Evidence after fix: Added 6 new unit tests covering the exact failure mode. The tests simulate an external file modification during the lock release window and verify the controller successfully restores the backup without throwing an error.

Observed result after fix: The attempt continues normally after a simulated takeover because the pre-prompt state is successfully restored. Backup files are cleanly removed upon completion or disposal.

What was not tested: Real-world concurrency races across multiple physical devices simultaneously accessing the same remote session file.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: Tested using Vitest's file I/O mocks to simulate the race conditions deterministically.

Tests and validation
Which commands did you run? pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts

What regression coverage was added or updated? Added 6 specific test cases to attempt.session-lock.test.ts:

creates backup before releasing lock for prompt
restores from backup on non-benign file change
cleans up backup after successful reacquire
throws takeover error when backup restore also fails
cleans up backup on dispose
What failed before this fix, if known? EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError was unconditionally thrown when assertSessionFileFence() detected a mismatch, terminating the process.

If no test was added, why not? Tests were added.

Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No) No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No) No.

What is the highest-risk area? File I/O operations (fs.copyFile) during backup and restore. If the process crashes mid-copy, a partial .prompt-bak file could be left behind.

How is that risk mitigated? The backup/restore flow is entirely best-effort wrapped in try/catch. If restoration fails or the backup is missing, the code safely falls back to the original behavior (throwing the EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError). Cleanup uses a silent fs.unlink that ignores ENOENT errors.

Current review state
What is the next action? Awaiting review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Needs CI pipeline execution to verify tests across all environments (Testbox/Crabbox).

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? N/A

target.title: fix(telegram): dead-letter ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND spooled updates instead of retrying
target.body:
## Summary

- Extend `resolveNonRetryableSpooledUpdateFailure` to classify `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors as non-retryable
- When `bot.handleUpdate` throws `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, the spooled update is now dead-lettered instead of being kept for retry
- Preserves retry for transient Telegram, filesystem, and network errors
- Adds `extractErrorCode` to the error graph traversal so the classifier can inspect `.code` properties in addition to `.name`

Fixes #92980. An `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` thrown from `bot.handleUpdate` is a permanent error — the loaded agent JavaScript bundle is missing and no retry can resolve it. Before this change the unresolved error returned `null` from the classifier and fell through to `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim`, creating a tight retry loop that blocked the spool queue and made the gateway appear frozen.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` thrown from `bot.handleUpdate` is not classified by `resolveNonRetryableSpooledUpdateFailure`, so it falls through to `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` and is kept for retry, creating a poison loop that blocks the Telegram spool queue. The fix adds `extractErrorCode` inspection to detect `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` in the error graph and dead-letters the update.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js, local git worktree based on `openclaw/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix:

```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/My Code/Github Code/openclaw-local

|extension-telegram| extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts (61 tests | 20 failed) 20040ms
|extension-telegram| extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts (8 tests | 0 failed)

 Test Files  1 failed | 1 passed (2)
      Tests  20 failed | 49 passed (69)
```

The 3 new tests pass (direct, cause-wrapped, and grammY BotError-wrapped `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` dead-lettered). The existing 3 missing-harness dead-letter tests continue to pass. The 20 failures are pre-existing Windows SQLite `EBUSY` file-lock issues unrelated to this change.

- Observed result after fix: `resolveNonRetryableSpooledUpdateFailure` returns `{ reason: "module-not-found", message }` for `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors, causing `#releaseFailedSpooledUpdate` to call `failTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` and dead-letter the update. Later same-lane updates drain normally without the poison update blocking the queue.
- What was not tested: A live Telegram gateway deployment was not exercised as part of this PR. The error shape used in tests (`new Error() + .code = "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"`) matches Node.js standard `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors thrown during dynamic imports.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Tests run on Windows where SQLite `better-sqlite3` native bindings cause `EBUSY` file-lock errors on cleanup (20 pre-existing failures). These are unrelated to this change and do not affect CI (which runs on Linux/macOS).

## Test coverage

| Test | Scenario |
|------|----------|
| `dead-letters ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures so later same-lane updates can drain` | Direct error with `.code = "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"` |
| `dead-letters cause-wrapped ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures` | Error wrapped via `{ cause }` |
| `dead-letters grammY BotError-wrapped ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures` | Error wrapped via BotError `.error` property |

All three tests verify:
- The poisoned update is dead-lettered (appears in failed queue)
- Later same-lane updates drain normally
- Log includes `"failed with non-retryable module-not-found"` and `"dead-lettered"`
- Log excludes `"keeping for retry"

target.title: test(cli): add banner emission reset helper
target.body:
Summary:
- Add a CLI banner testing reset helper for the module-level emission guard.
- Cover same-module reset/re-emit behavior in banner tests.

Fixes #83903

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: CLI banner module state can now be reset in the same loaded module instance, so tests that emit the banner can clear the one-shot guard and emit again.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24.14.0, dependencies installed with pnpm install --frozen-lockfile.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node --import tsx -e "const writes=[]; const originalWrite=process.stdout.write.bind(process.stdout); process.stdout.write=(chunk)=>{writes.push(String(chunk)); return true;}; Object.defineProperty(process.stdout,'isTTY',{configurable:true,value:true}); const {emitCliBanner,hasEmittedCliBanner,testing}=await import('./src/cli/banner.ts'); const options={argv:['node','openclaw'],commit:'abc1234',env:{LANG:'en_US.UTF-8'},isTty:true,mode:'off',platform:'darwin',richTty:false}; emitCliBanner('2026.3.7',options); if(!hasEmittedCliBanner()) throw new Error('banner was not marked emitted'); testing.resetBannerEmittedForTests(); if(hasEmittedCliBanner()) throw new Error('banner reset did not clear state'); emitCliBanner('2026.3.7',options); if(writes.length!==2) throw new Error('expected two writes after reset, got '+writes.length); process.stdout.write=originalWrite; console.log('ad hoc banner reset check passed');"
Evidence after fix: Console output from the real source checkout:
```
ad hoc banner reset check passed
```
Observed result after fix: The first emit set hasEmittedCliBanner() to true, resetBannerEmittedForTests() cleared it to false, and a second emit produced a second stdout write in the same module instance.
What was not tested: A packaged OpenClaw install was not launched; this PR only exposes a test reset helper and does not change production CLI banner emission behavior.

Verification:
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/banner.test.ts src/cli/channel-options.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/banner.ts src/cli/banner.test.ts
- git diff --check

target.title: fix(plugins): resolve openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports without root-alias.cjs fall-through (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Imports like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime` could fall through to `dist/plugin-sdk/root-alias.cjs/agent-harness-task-runtime` (a nested-under-a-file path that does not exist), breaking Codex/OpenClaw harness startup whenever a scoped subpath was not present as an exact entry in the loader alias map.

Why does this matter now?

- The fall-through breaks the agent harness boot path and any plugin that imports `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` for a subpath the alias map has not enumerated. The bug surfaces silently as a `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` rooted under `root-alias.cjs/...`, which is confusing to debug.

What is the intended outcome?

- `resolvePluginSdkScopedAliasMap` backfills scoped entries for real `dist/plugin-sdk/*.js` artifacts even when `package.json#exports` is stale, gated by `isUsableDistPluginSdkArtifact` (no broken artifacts) and `isBackfillableDistPluginSdkArtifactSubpath` (private SDK subpath gating preserved). `dist/plugin-sdk/index.js` is excluded since it's the root export, not a scoped subpath. Backfill honors `pluginSdkResolution` (src vs dist).
- `withNativeRequireAliases` resolves subpaths against the longest matching prefix, but **only when the alias target is a directory**. File-valued targets (e.g. the package-level `openclaw/plugin-sdk -> root-alias.cjs`) must match exactly via the scoped alias map; prefix fallback against a file target would bypass the same private-subpath gates the scoped map enforces. Suffix resolution uses `fs.statSync` for the file-vs-dir decision (falls back to `path.extname` if stat fails) and only swallows `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`/`ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Changes to `package.json#exports` and the SDK package contract itself. The scoped backfill is a runtime safety net; the canonical export list still drives the contract.
- The pre-existing TypeScript error in `src/secrets/config-io.ts` (unrelated to this fix).

What does success look like?

- Plugins requiring `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` resolve to the correct file. Private SDK subpaths remain inaccessible to untrusted callers. No alias value ever points inside `root-alias.cjs/<subpath>`. Source-mode dev/test loaders run src artifacts when src is preferred.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The native-require longest-prefix policy in `src/plugins/native-module-require.ts` (file-valued targets are excluded from prefix matching).
- The `orderedKinds`-aware backfill helper `resolveBackfillablePluginSdkSubpathTarget` in `src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts` and the `index`/private gating around `listDistPluginSdkArtifactSubpaths`.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No (proactive fix).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: file-valued root alias subpath fall-through producing `root-alias.cjs/<subpath>` paths and missing scoped aliases for stale `package.json#exports`.
- Real environment tested: vitest unit suite (91 tests across the three touched test files) + three scoped boundary lint scripts + `codex review --base origin/main` (final pass reports "No actionable regressions were identified").
- Exact steps or commands run after this patch:
    - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts src/plugins/native-module-require.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-module-loader-cache.test.ts`
    - `npm run lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`
    - `npm run lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal`
    - `npm run lint:plugins:no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports`
    - `codex review --base origin/main`
- Evidence after fix:
    - `RUN  v4.1.7 ... ✓ sdk-alias.test.ts (60 tests) ... ✓ native-module-require.test.ts (15 tests) ... ✓ plugin-module-loader-cache.test.ts (16 tests). Test Files 3 passed (3). Tests 91 passed (91).`
    - Lint output: `OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported.` / `No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found. Baseline matches (0 entries).` / `OK: bundled plugin source files use scoped plugin-sdk subpaths (4358 checked).`
    - Codex review final pass: `No actionable regressions were identified in the changed plugin SDK alias and native require paths.`
- Observed result after fix: Scoped alias map exposes `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime -> dist/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime.js` directly; `openclaw/plugin-sdk/index` is never published; private SDK subpaths (`codex-native-task-runtime`, `ssrf-runtime-internal`) remain undefined for untrusted modulePaths; `pluginSdkResolution: "src"` resolves to the src artifact when present.
- What was not tested: full end-to-end Codex/OpenClaw harness boot on a real install (this sandbox does not have a fully built openclaw runtime to exercise the harness against live transports). Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run because `pnpm` is not installed in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this PR was authored in a sandboxed agent environment (macOS, Node 25.8, npm 11.11, no pnpm, no real OpenClaw deploy). Maintainers/ClawSweeper should treat real-install verification as the next gate.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the original failure mode is documented in commit `cbb2968` and reproduced in `src/plugins/native-module-require.test.ts` (resolves scoped plugin-sdk subpaths via exact alias entries ... + does not resolve subpaths via prefix fallback against a file-valued root alias).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts src/plugins/native-module-require.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-module-loader-cache.test.ts` — PASS (91/91)
- `npm run lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported` — PASS
- `npm run lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal` — PASS
- `npm run lint:plugins:no-monolithic-plugin-sdk-entry-imports` — PASS
- `codex review --base origin/main` — three passes, final pass clean
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=12288" node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` — only pre-existing unrelated error in `src/secrets/config-io.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`: backfill respects exports staleness; private subpath gating preserved (`codex-native-task-runtime`, `ssrf-runtime-internal`); broken artifacts (`isUsableDistPluginSdkArtifact`-flagged) are skipped; `openclaw/plugin-sdk/index` is never published; backfill resolves to src when `pluginSdkResolution: "src"`; no alias value points inside `root-alias.cjs/<subpath>`.
- `src/plugins/native-module-require.test.ts`: longest-prefix only engages for directory-valued targets; exact-match scoped subpath alias works; file-valued root alias does NOT prefix-resolve to siblings (P2-1 gating regression); directory targets whose names contain a dot (e.g. `plugin-sdk.v2`) resolve correctly; non-`MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors propagate; missing subpath under file-valued root alias still falls back to original resolver cleanly.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `tryNativeRequireJavaScriptModule(..., aliasMap: { "openclaw/plugin-sdk": rootAliasPath })` against `require("openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime")` would fail with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` rooted under `root-alias.cjs/agent-harness-task-runtime`.

If no test was added, why not? N/A — 6 new regression tests added across both test files.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — plugins relying on `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath>` now resolve correctly; the file-valued-prefix native-require fallback (added in cbb2968 but never shipped to main) is intentionally restricted to directory-valued targets.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes (defense-in-depth)` — private SDK subpath gating (codex-native-task-runtime, codex-mcp-projection, ssrf-runtime-internal) is now reliably enforced for native-require resolution; the prior fallback could have bypassed it under contrived conditions.

What is the highest-risk area? `src/plugins/native-module-require.ts` `_resolveFilename` patching. It runs hot during every plugin require under `withNativeRequireAliases`. The new `isFileValuedAliasTarget` cache is module-scope; bounded by alias map size.

How is that risk mitigated? Memoized stat probe with `path.extname` fallback, narrow MODULE_NOT_FOUND catch, comprehensive test coverage (15 tests), and `codex review` cross-check.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review + real-OpenClaw-install proof from someone with access.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- External: real-environment harness boot verification (see Proof limitations).
- CI: full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` will run on the PR; could not be run locally due to missing `pnpm`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Internal Opus pre-submission review: gated backfill on `isUsableDistPluginSdkArtifact`, added private-gating regression test, narrowed error catch in `resolveAliasSubpathTarget`, fs.statSync file-vs-dir probe, renamed helper to `isBackfillableDistPluginSdkArtifactSubpath`, added Windows-dot-dir + re-throw + end-to-end regression tests.
- Codex review pass #1: restricted native-require prefix-based fallback to directory-valued alias targets (private-gating bypass fix); excluded `openclaw/plugin-sdk/index` from backfill.
- Codex review pass #2: `resolveBackfillablePluginSdkSubpathTarget` now walks `orderedKinds` so source-mode loaders no longer resolve backfilled subpaths to stale dist artifacts.
- Codex review pass #3: clean.

---

AI-assisted: this PR was authored with [Oz](https://warp.dev/oz). All four commits are signed with the `Co-Authored-By: Oz <oz-agent@warp.dev>` trailer. Codex was run locally three times against `origin/main` per CONTRIBUTING.md guidance for AI-assisted PRs.

_Conversation: https://app.warp.dev/conversation/2d289493-3706-484b-8d65-da0e5ce2cd46_
_Run: https://oz.warp.dev/runs/019e66cf-cb04-7a54-8475-3409158dff6b_

_This PR was generated with [Oz](https://warp.dev/oz)._
