target.title: fix: relay Claude CLI assistant partial messages as streaming deltas
target.body:
Fixes #86050

## Summary

Relay Claude CLI assistant records as streaming deltas so the WebChat UI shows progressive output instead of waiting for the full response.

**Fix 1**: Parse `assistant` records from the Claude CLI JSONL stream and forward their text content as `chat.delta` events.

**Fix 2**: Gate on `stop_reason` using a positive partial signal: only forward records where `stop_reason` is explicitly present and `null` (the documented Claude CLI partial marker). Complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped to avoid duplicating the final message. Records without `stop_reason` at all are ignored as ambiguous (could be complete messages from custom `claude-stream-json` backends).

## Changed files

- `src/agents/cli-output.ts` -- Added `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` with positive `stop_reason: null` guard
- `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` -- Tests covering partial forwarding, complete-message skip, absent-stop_reason rejection, and edge cases

## Verification

All tests use the documented `stop_reason: null` marker for partial messages:
- `stop_reason: null` (explicit) -> text forwarded as streaming delta
- `stop_reason: "end_turn"` -> record skipped (complete message, not a partial)
- `stop_reason` absent -> record skipped (not a confirmed partial; prevents false delivery from custom backends)
- Non-assistant record types -> ignored by the parser
- Malformed content arrays -> returns null without throwing
- Mixed text + tool_use content -> text extracted, tool_use entries ignored

Fixes #86050

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude CLI assistant records are not relayed as streaming deltas, so WebChat waits for the full response. The `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function requires a positive partial signal (`stop_reason: null` explicitly present) to skip complete messages and forward only true streaming partials as `chat.delta` events.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from branch `fix/gateway-stream-relay` at `/tmp/proof-86649`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify the partial assistant delta parser is active in compiled production code.
Step 2. Feed four Claude CLI JSONL records through the production `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`: two partials (`stop_reason: null`), one complete (`stop_reason: "end_turn"`), and one ambiguous (no `stop_reason` field).

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The partial assistant delta parser and its `stop_reason: null` guard are active in the compiled production bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta\|stop_reason.*null\|partialDelta" dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js
216:function parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta(params) {
220:if (!isRecord$1(message) || !("stop_reason" in message) || message.stop_reason !== null) return null;
394:const partialDelta = parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta({
402:if (partialDelta) {
403:assistantText = partialDelta.text;
404:params.onAssistantDelta(partialDelta);
```

Runtime exercise of the production parser with Claude CLI JSONL event shapes:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-exercise.ts
=== Feeding Claude CLI JSONL records through production parser ===

Input record 1 (partial, stop_reason: null):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"}]

Input record 2 (partial, stop_reason: null, extended text):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]

Input record 3 (complete, stop_reason: "end_turn"):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer to your question."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Complete message skipped (no new delta), avoiding duplicate delivery

Input record 4 (no stop_reason field, ambiguous source):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Some response"}]},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Record without stop_reason skipped (not a confirmed partial)

=== Summary ===
Total records fed: 4
Partial deltas relayed: 2
Delta breakdown:
  [0] delta="Here is" (cumulative: "Here is")
  [1] delta=" the answer" (cumulative: "Here is the answer")

Before fix: Records 1-2 would not emit deltas (assistant type was ignored).
After fix: Records 1-2 relay as streaming deltas. Records 3-4 correctly skipped.
No duplicate final text delivered.
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production code at `dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js:216-220` contains the `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function with the positive partial signal guard (`stop_reason !== null`). At line 394, the main parser calls this function as a fallback when no raw stream delta is available. Runtime exercise confirms: assistant records with `stop_reason: null` relay as incremental streaming deltas (`"Here is"` then `" the answer"`), complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped (preventing duplicate delivery), and ambiguous records without `stop_reason` are also skipped (preventing false delivery from custom backends).

What was not tested: Live Claude CLI session with real streaming output over the gateway WebSocket connection. The fix exercises the production parser against the documented Claude CLI JSONL protocol shapes. End-to-end WebChat/TUI display was not verified.

target.title: fix(ui): deduplicate assistant messages on chat.status final event
target.body:
Fixes #85755

## Summary

Fix duplicate assistant messages appearing in WebChat when `loadChatHistory` and the `final` chat event race.

**Guard**: `isTailDuplicate` uses `messageDisplaySignature` to detect when the same message arrives via both history load and the live event stream.

**Cross-run preservation**: The dedup guard is applied only to same-run final/aborted events (the real race condition). Cross-run finals (sub-agent announcements, separate runs) are always appended because they may legitimately produce the same text. The renderer's existing `duplicateCount` collapse handles visual dedup for consecutive identical messages.

**Stale stream fallback prevention**: When a duplicate terminal payload is detected (final or aborted), the message is treated as fully handled. The code no longer falls through to the stream fallback, which could contain stale or different text from an earlier delta burst.

## Changed files

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` - `isTailDuplicate` helper, guards on same-run final/aborted/stream-fallback paths; cross-run path always appends; duplicate terminal payloads skip stream fallback
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` - 7 new tests: same-run dedup, cross-run preservation, stream-fallback dedup, aborted dedup, stale-stream-on-final-duplicate, stale-stream-on-aborted-duplicate

## Verification

```
Terminal: pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts

 ✓ |unit-ui| ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts (98 tests) 22ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  98 passed (98)
```

Behavioral checks:
- Same-run final with matching tail -> skipped (race condition dedup)
- Same-run final duplicate + stale chatStream -> stream text NOT appended (P2 fix)
- Same-run aborted duplicate + stale chatStream -> stream text NOT appended (P2 fix)
- Cross-run final with matching tail -> appended (legitimate repeat, renderer handles display collapse)
- Stream-fallback with matching tail -> skipped (same-run race)
- Aborted message with matching tail -> skipped (same-run race)
- Different content -> always appended regardless of run

Closes #85771

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** (1) Duplicate assistant messages appear in WebChat when `loadChatHistory` and the `final` chat event race. The `isTailDuplicate` guard detects when the same message arrives via both paths. (2) When the duplicate terminal message was detected, the code previously fell through to the stream fallback. If `chatStream` contained a different partial or stale value from an earlier delta burst, that stale text was appended instead of treating the duplicate terminal as fully handled. Both the `final` and `aborted` branches had this issue.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node 26.0.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `/tmp/wt-86646`. UI code exercises the production `handleChatEvent` function from `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` via `npx tsx`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1. Ran the full test suite to confirm all 98 tests pass (including 2 new stale-stream tests):

```bash
cd /tmp/wt-86646 && npx vitest run ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
```

Step 2. Ran a proof script that imports the production `handleChatEvent` and exercises three scenarios:

```bash
cd /tmp/wt-86646 && npx tsx /tmp/proof-86646.ts
```

The proof script creates real `ChatState` objects with stale `chatStream` values and duplicate terminal payloads, then asserts the stale stream text is never appended.

- **Evidence after fix:**

Test suite output (98 tests, all passing):

```console
$ npx vitest run ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
 ✓ |unit-ui| ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts (98 tests) 22ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  98 passed (98)
```

Production proof output:

```console
$ npx tsx /tmp/proof-86646.ts

=== Test 1: duplicate final with stale chatStream ===
  PASS  returns 'final'
  PASS  chatMessages length stays 1
  PASS  stale stream text NOT appended
  PASS  original message preserved

=== Test 2: duplicate aborted with stale chatStream ===
  PASS  returns 'aborted'
  PASS  chatMessages length stays 1
  PASS  stale stream text NOT appended
  PASS  original message preserved

=== Test 3: cross-run final still appends new message ===
  PASS  chatMessages length is 2
  PASS  new cross-run message appended
  PASS  active stream preserved

=== Summary: 11 passed, 0 failed ===
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The production `handleChatEvent` correctly handles duplicate terminal payloads without falling through to the stale stream fallback. When a final or aborted payload matches the chat tail (history-load/final-event race), the message is treated as "already visible" and skipped, with no stream text appended. When a cross-run final arrives from a different runId, it is always appended regardless of tail content. The fix restructures the conditional nesting so that `isTailDuplicate` returning true stays within the "valid terminal message" branch instead of falling through to `else if (chatStream)`.

- **What was not tested:** Live WebChat UI with a real gateway session producing the history-load/final-event race condition with a stale `chatStream` value.

target.title: fix(tavily): pass api_key in request body instead of Authorization header
target.body:
## Problem

When Tavily is configured as the web search provider (`tools.web.search.provider: "tavily"`) with a valid API key, all `web_search` calls return **0 results** with no error message. This affects all agents including family bots, security research, and any workflow using web search.

### Root Cause

OpenClaw sends the Tavily API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header. Tavily's API **silently ignores** this header — it expects `api_key` in the request body. Since no error is returned, the search "succeeds" with `count: 0`.

The `postTrustedWebToolsJson` helper always puts the key in an `Authorization` header, which works for Brave/Perplexity but not Tavily.

### Fix

In `postTavilyJson`, spread `api_key` into the request body before forwarding:

```diff
- body: params.body,
+ body: { ...params.body, api_key: params.apiKey },
```

This ensures the API key reaches Tavily in the format it expects.

### Verification

- **Before:** `web_search("Metformin side effects")` → `count: 0` (no error)
- **After:** `web_search("Metformin side effects")` → `count: 3` (NHS, GoodRx, etc.) in 64ms

Direct API test confirms Tavily works with `api_key` in body but returns empty when only Bearer auth is used.

target.title: fix(imessage): honor disabled reply actions
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92142 by making the iMessage sender honor `channels.imessage.actions.reply === false` before preserving outbound reply metadata.

When reply actions are disabled, `sendMessageIMessage` now drops `replyToId` before it can become the `imsg` RPC `reply_to` parameter or `send-attachment --reply-to`. That lets final iMessage responses degrade to normal top-level sends on SIP-enabled macOS setups instead of entering the SIP-gated threaded reply path.

## Root cause

Final delivery passes `payload.replyToId` into the iMessage sender for text and media replies. The sender previously sanitized and reused that value unconditionally, so `actions.reply: false` disabled advertised/private reply actions but not the lower-level final delivery metadata.

## Changes

- Gate resolved iMessage reply metadata with the existing `createActionGate(account.config.actions)("reply")` helper.
- Add regression coverage for disabled reply actions on text sends and media attachment sends.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` passed: 43 tests.
- `pnpm --silent exec oxfmt --check extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` passed.

## Duplicate PRs

This maintainer PR supersedes #92170 and #92920. Both contributor PRs target the same issue and same sender line; this PR keeps the maintainer-owned fix while borrowing the stronger sender-boundary test shape.

target.title: feat(zalouser): add groupLink action to fetch a group's invite link
target.body:
## What
Adds a `groupLink` action to the zalouser tool that returns a group's shareable invite link. The tool can already `groups` (list) and `link` (send a URL as a message), but there was no way to read a group's invite link.

## Changes
- `zca-client.ts`: add `getGroupLinkDetail(groupId)` to the `API` interface.
- `zalo-js.ts`: add `getZaloGroupInviteLink(profile, groupId)` wrapping it via `withZaloApi`, returning `{ link?, expiresAt?, enabled }`.
- `tool.ts`: add the `groupLink` action (resolves the group thread, returns the link detail).
- `tool.test.ts`: success + disabled-link + missing-group-id cases.

## Why getGroupLinkDetail (not enableGroupLink)
`enableGroupLink` toggles the invite-link feature on, which is admin-only — a regular member account gets a permission error. `getGroupLinkDetail` reads the existing link, so it works for any member once a group admin has enabled the link. When the link is not enabled the action returns `{ enabled: false }` and the caller degrades cleanly.

## Use case
Lets an agent (e.g. a customer-service hand-off flow) surface a link so a human can join the relevant group.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: the groupLink action originally called the admin-only enableGroupLink, so a regular (non-admin) group member hit a Zalo permission error ("Không đủ quyền") and never got a link. After switching to the read-only getGroupLinkDetail, a non-admin member reads an already-enabled invite link.

Real environment tested: a real Zalo personal account signed in as a regular member (not an admin) of the target groups, running inside a live OpenClaw agent container. The bundled @openclaw/zalouser plugin was hot-patched on disk to mirror this branch and the gateway was restarted so the patched module loaded.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: docker exec <agent> openclaw agent --message "Call the zalouser tool with action=groupLink, threadId=<group id> and return the raw JSON result" --json — run against three real groups (two with the invite link enabled, one with it disabled), driving the patched groupLink action end-to-end through tool.ts to zalo-js to zca-js getGroupLinkDetail.

Evidence after fix: live output copied from the running container for an enabled group (invite-link token redacted; it is a real join link to a private group):
```json
{ "link": "https://zalo.me/g/<redacted>", "expiresAt": 1937494664737, "enabled": true }
```
the same openclaw groupLink call against a group whose invite link was disabled returned, with no error:
```json
{ "enabled": false }
```

Observed result after fix: across three real groups the non-admin member account read invite links via getGroupLinkDetail — two enabled groups returned valid https://zalo.me/g/<token> links with an expiry, and the disabled group returned { enabled: false }. With the previous enableGroupLink call every one of those groups returned "Không đủ quyền". The docker exec openclaw run printed the JSON shown above verbatim.

What was not tested: the admin-only enableGroupLink toggle path (out of scope — this PR is read-only); the downstream hand-off message wiring that consumes the link lives in a separate consumer, not this PR.

target.title: fix(telegram): ignore polling sleep gaps
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Telegram polling liveness so the first watchdog tick after a long sleep/resume or event-loop pause resets the active `getUpdates` baseline instead of forcing a false active-stall restart.
- Keeps active in-flight elapsed time on a monotonic clock for normal watchdog intervals while preserving wall-clock timestamps for diagnostics and idle polling detection.
- Adds regression coverage for a 10-hour wall-clock and monotonic jump after a started poll.
- Out of scope: live Telegram credential/provider reruns.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #86535

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #86535

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Local bug evidence under `/mnt/c/OpenClaw/bugs/BUG-038-telegram-polling-stall-detector-treats-long-sleep-as-active-getupdates-stall`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Telegram polling no longer classifies a long sleep/resume gap as an active stuck `getUpdates` request on the first watchdog tick after resume.
- Real environment tested: Linux WSL2 dev checkout, Node 24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.25 branch `bug-038-telegram-polling-sleep`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-liveness.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```shell
RUN  v4.1.7 /home/galini/GitHub/worktrees/bug-038-telegram-polling-sleep

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
  Start at  15:13:59
  Duration  784ms

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

- Observed result after fix: The regression test advances wall-clock and monotonic time by 10 hours after a started poll and `detectStall()` returns `null` for the first post-resume check; a later normal active interval still reports `active getUpdates stuck`.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram polling with real credentials was not rerun.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The observed production evidence came from redacted gateway logs; local proof covers the liveness seam without private Telegram credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```shell
[telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 37504.36s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=[timestamp] finishedAt=[same timestamp] durationMs=30668 offset=161256658]
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-liveness.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/telegram/src/polling-liveness.test.ts` now covers long sleep/resume gaps for active polling.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Redacted logs showed active-stall restarts after 2.4 to 10.4 hours even though the poll duration was about 30 seconds.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

Telegram polling stall detection.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change only suppresses active-stall detection after a watchdog gap larger than twice the stall threshold; continuous active stalls still fail the existing regression.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Live Telegram proof would require private credentials and was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Autoreview initially flagged platform sleep-clock assumptions; this PR now detects long watchdog gaps instead and the rerun was clean.

target.title: fix: preserve subagent delivery after lock stalls
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves suspended subagent final-delivery text into durable completion fallback state before expiring the outbox payload, so expiry is not a hard loss of the child result.
- Distinguishes expiry after durable fallback preservation from true payload discard.
- Adds session write-lock timeout owner diagnostics including liveness, starttime, current starttime, age, and stale reasons.
- Out of scope: redesigning subagent scheduling or session JSONL lock ownership.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #86537
Closes #86538

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #86537
Related #86538

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Local bug evidence under `/mnt/c/OpenClaw/bugs/BUG-035-subagent-terminal-reconciliation-times-out-then-discards-delivery` and `/mnt/c/OpenClaw/bugs/BUG-036-session-write-lock-timeouts-block-main-and-subagent-lanes`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Expired suspended subagent final delivery now keeps final text durably instead of dropping the only payload, and write-lock timeouts expose actionable owner diagnostics.
- Real environment tested: Linux WSL2 dev checkout plus Azure Crabbox remote validation. Before proof used Azure Crabbox `silver-prawn` / `cbx_e06ef86ae274`; after proof used Azure Crabbox `violet-lobster` / `cbx_3eda7a42d715`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; Azure Crabbox before/after focused regression proof.

target.title: Fix voice-call trusted proxy matching for IPv4-mapped webhooks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86525.

Normalizes voice-call trusted proxy IP comparisons before webhook URL reconstruction decides whether forwarded host/proto headers are trusted. This aligns Twilio/Plivo webhook signature verification with the existing media-stream client-IP path when Node reports a trusted IPv4 proxy as an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address.

## Motivation / root cause

`reconstructWebhookUrl` compared `trustedProxyIPs` to the socket remote address with a literal string match. A config value of `127.0.0.1` therefore did not match `::ffff:127.0.0.1`, so valid Twilio/Plivo callbacks behind a loopback proxy ignored `X-Forwarded-Host` and reconstructed `https://localhost/...` instead of the provider-signed public URL.

## User-visible behavior

Voice-call webhooks behind local or loopback reverse proxies continue to verify provider signatures when Node reports the proxy peer in IPv4-mapped form. Forwarded headers are still ignored unless `allowedHosts` is configured or `trustForwardingHeaders` is explicitly enabled, and the request still has to come from a trusted proxy when `trustedProxyIPs` is set.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: valid Twilio/Plivo webhook signatures were rejected when the request remote address was `::ffff:127.0.0.1` but `trustedProxyIPs` contained `127.0.0.1`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node v24.10.0 with pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script calling `verifyTwilioWebhook` with `remoteAddress: "::ffff:127.0.0.1"`, `trustedProxyIPs: ["127.0.0.1"]`, `x-forwarded-host: proxy.example.com`, and a valid Twilio signature for `https://proxy.example.com/voice/webhook`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "verificationUrl": "https://proxy.example.com/voice/webhook",
  "isReplay": false,
  "verifiedRequestKey": "twilio:req:56cf1e50f664e1464033f16c08ad96ce0a96b29597f4be9126eb8f1d0029412a"
}
```

Observed result after fix: the verifier trusts the forwarded public URL only for the normalized trusted proxy and accepts the valid signature.

What was not tested: no live Twilio, Plivo, or reverse-proxy E2E was run; this is local runtime proof plus focused verifier tests.

## Regression tests

Added focused tests covering both provider signature paths:

- Twilio signed webhook with `remoteAddress: "::ffff:127.0.0.1"` and `trustedProxyIPs: ["127.0.0.1"]`.
- Plivo V3 signed webhook with the same trusted proxy normalization shape.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.test.ts` - 26 tests passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` - lanes: `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
- `pnpm check:changed` - passed.
- `git diff --check -- extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook-security.test.ts` - passed.
- Claude Review for Codex, model `opus 4.7` - approved; no material findings.
- Codex code review - no blocking issues.
- Codex Security diff review - no actionable security findings.

## Compatibility and risks

Compatibility: this keeps existing exact trusted proxy config working and only canonicalizes equivalent IPv4-mapped/plain IPv4 peer representations. It does not add CIDR support or change the documented forwarding-header opt-in requirements.

Risk: low. The changed condition is security-sensitive, but forwarded headers are still gated by the same `allowedHosts` / `trustForwardingHeaders` requirement and the normalized socket peer match.

target.title: fix(telegram): render progress drafts as rich previews
target.body:
### Summary Telegram progress drafts now render from structured progress lines into a caller-provided rich preview instead of forcing Telegram spacing through the shared plain-text formatter. This removes the PR's `lineSeparator` special case from core. Core still composes portable draft text with normal newlines; Telegram uses the same structured `ChannelProgressDraftLine` objects to build `sendRichMessage` / `editMessageText` HTML with explicit `
` breaks. ### Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** streamed Telegram tool-progress entries collapsed together when rich markdown treated single newlines as soft breaks. - **Real environment tested:** live Telegram bot-to-bot run from this PR branch, using a temporary OpenClaw gateway, the Telegram plugin's real draft stream path, and Bot API rich messages. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node /Users/obviyus/.codex/skills/custom/telegram-e2e-bot-to-bot/scripts/run-mock-sut-e2e.mjs --text '@{sut} mcp code mode api file qa check. Return the OK marker from the tool result.' --expect Exec --any-sut-reply --timeout-ms 90000 --output /tmp/openclaw-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124-retry.json ``` - **Evidence after fix:** copied live terminal output from the passing Telegram run: ```text ok: true sent.messageId: 32453 sent.text: @foremanclawbot mcp code mode api file qa check. Return the OK marker from the tool result. reply.messageId: 32454 reply.fromUsername: foremanclawbot reply.replyToMessageId: 32453 reply.text excerpt: Scuttling 🛠️ Exec running 🛠️ Exec Validation failed for tool "exec": ... failed providerRequests: 2 gatewayLogTail: "" ``` - **Observed result after fix:** the real Telegram reply is threaded to the driver message and the progress rows are separated instead of being joined into one long monospace line. - **What was not tested:** broad/full CI locally. A first live attempt hit a shared Telegram `getUpdates` token conflict, then the same progress probe passed on retry. ### Verification - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124.tsbuildinfo` - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-telegram-rich-progress-pr93124.tsbuildinfo` - `git diff --check` - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts src/channels/streaming.ts` - Normal Telegram reply smoke: sent `32457`, observed `32458` with `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK`, provider requests: 1.

target.title: [codex] mirror proactive deliveries into session transcripts
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror proactive cron announce/failure delivery into custom/main session transcripts with a stable `cron-announce-mirror:v1:*` idempotency key.
- Keep routed direct, legacy dm, group, and channel session transcripts out of cron mirrors, including provider-owned suffixes such as Telegram `:topic:` and Slack thread suffixes.
- Mirror heartbeat/digest delivery into the active/base chat transcript with a stable `heartbeat-delivery-mirror:v1:*` idempotency key so follow-up replies can see the proactive message that was actually sent.
- Add focused regression coverage for both delivery paths.

Supersedes #86140 with the same bug class handled in one PR.

## Verification
- Based on `origin/main` `5f934830d38272dbe5ed12b453430599378dd936`; PR head is `0a806a46410479c718e79884c8cb9148c84645b9`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/delivery.failure-notify.test.ts` (1 file, 13 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts` (1 file, 14 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/delivery.ts src/cron/delivery.failure-notify.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` (`core`, `coreTests`)
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` temp-state cron alias proof (`target_session_key=main`, `canonical_session_key=agent:ops:work`, `alias_mirror_ok=true`)
- Added heartbeat text+mediaUrls regression coverage: transcript mirrors preserve the delivered heartbeat text instead of resolving to the attachment filename.

## Changelog candidate
- Proactive delivery: mirror cron announce/failure notifications and heartbeat/digest sends into the destination transcript so follow-up replies keep the delivered message in context.

Note: the changelog line is intentionally kept out of this PR diff to avoid repeated `CHANGELOG.md` conflicts while `main` is moving quickly. It can be added at landing.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Proactive outbound deliveries that are visible to a user now append the delivered assistant text to the transcript that the next turn will read. Cron announce/failure delivery mirrors only into custom/main session keys and skips routed direct/dm/group/channel chat transcripts, including provider-owned suffixes such as Telegram topics. Heartbeat/digest delivery mirrors into the active/base chat session key, so a reply after a WhatsApp-style proactive digest has the sent digest in context.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout `/Users/dross/openclaw-cron-transcript-mirror`, branch `codex/proactive-delivery-transcript-mirrors`, head `0a806a46410479c718e79884c8cb9148c84645b9`, based on `origin/main` `5f934830d38272dbe5ed12b453430599378dd936`, with a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. The proof uses a local in-process outbound plugin named `proofline`, so no private external account, phone number, endpoint, or token is needed. It exercises `sendCronAnnouncePayloadStrict` and `runHeartbeatOnce` through `sendDurableMessageBatch`, outbound delivery, and transcript append.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` from the repo root with an inline harness that registered a local `proofline` outbound adapter, seeded the default session store with a custom cron session key, a Telegram topic-suffixed group session key, and a direct heartbeat session key, delivered cron and heartbeat messages, then read the resulting transcript files from the temporary state directory.

Evidence after fix:

```text
OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_PROOF proactive delivery transcript mirrors
state_dir=/var/folders/69/5f9v9rq9045bwk3w75cht7wm0000gn/T/openclaw-proactive-mirror-proof.VAikaS
store_path=/var/folders/69/5f9v9rq9045bwk3w75cht7wm0000gn/T/openclaw-proactive-mirror-proof.VAikaS/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
send_results=[{"to":"cron-recipient","text":"proof cron mirror text"},{"to":"topic-recipient","text":"proof topic mirror skip text"},{"to":"heartbeat-user","text":"proof heartbeat mirror text"}]
cron_custom_transcript={"exists":true,"lineCount":2,"lastRole":"assistant","lastText":"proof cron mirror text","lastIdempotencyKey":null}
topic_group_transcript={"exists":false,"lineCount":0,"lastRole":null,"lastText":null,"lastIdempotencyKey":null}
heartbeat_transcript={"exists":true,"lineCount":2,"lastRole":"assistant","lastText":"proof heartbeat mirror text","lastIdempotencyKey":null}
cron_mirror_ok=true
topic_group_mirror_skipped=true
heartbeat_mirror_ok=true
```

Observed result after fix: All three sends reached the local outbound adapter. The custom cron session transcript was created and ended with the mirrored assistant message. The Telegram topic-suffixed group session still delivered but did not create a transcript mirror. The direct heartbeat session transcript was created and ended with the mirrored heartbeat/digest assistant message.

What was not tested: I did not run a real WhatsApp, Telegram, or Slack network send from this branch. The motivating live WhatsApp evidence was captured separately on a private Dross setup: a heartbeat/digest message was visible in WhatsApp, the user replied immediately after it, and the active session transcript did not contain the digest before this patch. That before-fix evidence is privacy-redacted and not attached as a PR image.

target.title: Redesign Overview as an operator-first OpenClaw dashboard
target.body:
## Summary
- Rework the connected Overview page into an operator-first dashboard instead of a setup/status landing page.
- Keep first-run Gateway Access and Snapshot cards intact for disconnected installs, then move them below daily operating signals once the Control UI is connected.
- Use existing gateway, session, cron, auth, provider/quota, connector, skill, and log data. This PR intentionally avoids adding new Gateway RPCs.

## Major functional improvements
- Makes the Overview useful at a glance: the first screen now answers what needs attention, what just ran, whether model/provider usage looks healthy, and whether cron/connectors/skills/logs look normal.
- Reduces dashboard scrolling: Attention and Recent Sessions stop growing without bound, and the usage/quota surface now uses a compact metric layout instead of a tall card.
- Respects OpenClaw's multi-model posture: the dashboard presents provider/quota health from the data available in the install, without implying OpenClaw is tied to one LLM provider.
- Shows clearer provider-limit detail when providers expose different kinds of limits: time buckets, model-tier buckets, token budgets, credit balances, and monthly quotas use provider-reported labels instead of a generic "quota" tile.
- Protects private identifiers in recent sessions: long digit runs are blurred again, including phone-number-like iMessage session IDs.
- Improves disconnected behavior: setup-oriented cards still show when Gateway data is not available, so first-run and broken-connection flows do not regress.
- Handles quiet or partial installs cleanly: empty sessions, missing cron jobs, api-key-only auth, absent quota data, and unavailable logs all get deliberate fallback states.

## Review fixes
- Restores digit-run blurring for connected Overview recent-session names and adds a regression fixture for a numeric iMessage-style direct-session identifier.
- Routes the new operator-dashboard user-facing copy through Control UI i18n keys and syncs shipped locale bundles/metadata.
- Replaces the Codex-specific usage card title with provider/model usage wording while still showing concrete provider names and provider-reported quota labels when providers supply usage data.
- Uses quota data from any provider that reports it, including API-key providers, while keeping the separate auth-health card focused on OAuth/token providers that can expire. Self-describing labels like OpenRouter `Credits` are no longer forced into `Credits quota`.
- Applies the same provider-native quota formatting to the chat header pill, so OpenRouter-style `Credits` renders with native remaining labels such as `$12.34 left` instead of `Credits quota` plus a normalized percentage.
- Suppresses the local Cost metric when it would only show `$0.00` beside provider-native quota/credit data, and folds useful provider details such as `$8.90 used of $21.24` into the quota cell instead.
- Adds provider-native quota display labels to the shared usage model, so balance providers can show dollar/credit amounts while percentage fields still support sorting and pressure ranking.
- Adds OpenRouter usage fetching for `/api/v1/credits` and `/api/v1/key`, displaying account credits/key limits as dollar balances when OpenRouter returns them.
- Tightens the first-screen layout by capping Attention/Recent Sessions rows and making the usage/quota card a compact metric grid.

## Screenshots
Sanitized Codex-plan Provider Usage render from the current branch UI. This is a synthetic fixture using generic sessions and localhost-only connection data, so it shows the real component state without exposing a personal install:

![Sanitized Codex provider quota Overview render](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-codex-quota-redacted.png)

Sanitized OpenRouter-style Provider Usage render from the same current branch UI. It shows provider-native dollar credit display alongside other provider quota shapes without claiming a live OpenRouter account was queried locally:

![Sanitized OpenRouter credits Overview render](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/homer-byte/openclaw/pr-assets/86460/.github/pr-assets/86460/openclaw-overview-openrouter-quota-redacted.png)

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts extensions/openrouter/usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:sync`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `pnpm lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Connected Control UI Overview should show the compact operator dashboard while preserving privacy for recent-session identifiers, avoiding unbounded first-screen card height, and presenting model/provider usage without making OpenClaw look Codex-only or forcing credit/balance providers into percentage-only labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Current branch Control UI rendered through a local Vite/Playwright fixture at 1440x1200. Fixture data is synthetic and sanitized: generic sessions, localhost connection settings, no personal names, no account identifiers, no phone numbers, and no secrets.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built and tested the UI, rendered the Overview component with Codex-plan and OpenRouter-style provider usage fixtures, captured screenshots with Playwright, checked the PNGs for obvious personal/secret strings, pushed refreshed PNGs to the PR asset branch, and updated the PR body.
- **Evidence after fix:** The Codex render shows a zero-cost plan setup where the Cost metric is replaced by same-provider quota windows:

```text
WEEK QUOTA      85% left   reset 5d 2h
5H QUOTA        90% left   reset 2h 8m
MESSAGES        146
TOKENS          184k
```

The OpenRouter render shows provider-native credit formatting rather than forcing a percentage-only display:

```text
CREDITS         $12.34 left
                $8.90 used of $21.24
MESSAGES        146
TOKENS          184k
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The screenshots show the Provider Usage card adapting to both Codex plan quotas and OpenRouter dollar credits while using the same current branch component/CSS. The data is realistic provider-shaped fixture data, not a personal live-account capture.
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenRouter account balance was queried on the local install; the OpenRouter screenshot remains a clearly labeled fixture. Broader cross-browser visual QA remains ordinary maintainer review scope.

## Implementation notes
- This PR intentionally avoids new Gateway RPCs. Message queue state, update posture, and host-health exceptions are represented only when existing dashboard data can support them.
- Follow-up telemetry could make the dashboard stronger by exposing pending sends/retries, installed-vs-latest version, restart-needed state, broader provider/quota signals, and host capacity alerts directly.

target.title: fix: preserve BOOTSTRAP.md in preseeded workspaces (#91931)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #91931: preseeded `SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `USER.md` no longer cause bootstrap auto-completion that deletes user-provided `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first onboarding.
- When `BOOTSTRAP.md` exists and `setupCompletedAt` is unset, completion evidence now requires concrete user content (`memory/`, `.git`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`) instead of profile-file diffs alone.
- Preserves existing behavior for workspaces that already completed setup or lack `BOOTSTRAP.md`.

Why does this matter now?

GitOps / operator deployments preseed workspace profile files before the first user-visible onboarding run.

What is the intended outcome?

Managed workspaces keep `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real onboarding evidence exists.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Changing default template contents for fresh non-preseeded workspaces.

What does success look like?

`ensureAgentWorkspace({ ensureBootstrapFiles: true })` on a preseeded workspace leaves `BOOTSTRAP.md` present and does not set `setupCompletedAt`.

What should reviewers focus on?

`workspaceHasBootstrapCompletionEvidence` branch when `bootstrapExists && !setupCompletedAt`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91931

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related operator/GitOps preseeded workspace bootstrap behavior.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — community bug report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Preseeded custom profile files must not auto-complete bootstrap and delete `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first onboarding.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11; Node 24 with `node --import tsx`; temp workspace under `%TEMP%`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd openclaw
node --import tsx -e "
import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
import path from 'node:path';
import os from 'node:os';
import {
  ensureAgentWorkspace,
  DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_SOUL_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_IDENTITY_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_USER_FILENAME,
} from './src/agents/workspace.ts';

const tempDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'openclaw-preseed-proof-'));
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_SOUL_FILENAME), '# SOUL.md\n\nCustom platform soul\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_IDENTITY_FILENAME), '# IDENTITY.md\n\nCustom platform identity\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_USER_FILENAME), '# USER.md\n\nCustom platform user\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME), '# BOOTSTRAP.md\n\nFirst-run onboarding flow\n');
await ensureAgentWorkspace({ dir: tempDir, ensureBootstrapFiles: true });
const bootstrapExists = await fs.access(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME)).then(() => true).catch(() => false);
const state = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(path.join(tempDir, '.openclaw', 'workspace-state.json'), 'utf8'));
console.log('BOOTSTRAP.md exists:', bootstrapExists);
console.log('workspace-state:', JSON.stringify(state, null, 2));
"
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
fresh preseed: BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true; setupCompletedAt unset
restart-before-onboarding: BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true; setupCompletedAt unset; reconcile.repaired: false
{
  "freshPreseedBootstrapExists": true,
  "freshState": { "version": 1, "bootstrapSeededAt": "2026-06-15T00:07:06.553Z" },
  "restartBootstrapExists": true,
  "restartState": { "version": 1, "bootstrapSeededAt": "2026-06-15T00:07:06.553Z" },
  "reconcileRepaired": false
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Fresh preseed keeps `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real onboarding; restart-before-first-onboarding with preseeded profile files also keeps `BOOTSTRAP.md` pending (profile diffs alone never complete bootstrap while `setupCompletedAt` is unset).
- What was not tested: Full Kubernetes/operator deployment path end-to-end; only the workspace bootstrap reconciliation code path was exercised locally.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Reproduced via direct `ensureAgentWorkspace` call rather than a long-running gateway pod; supplemental vitest regression also passes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): On affected main behavior, preseeded profile diffs alone could mark bootstrap complete and remove `BOOTSTRAP.md` (see issue #91931 repro).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- Live repro script above (node --import tsx) → `BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/workspace.test.ts -t "BOOTSTRAP|bootstrap|preseeded|stale"` → 42 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/workspace.test.ts -t "preserves BOOTSTRAP.md in preseeded"` → passed

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Fresh preseed: `preserves BOOTSTRAP.md in preseeded workspaces with custom profile files`
- Restart-before-onboarding: `keeps BOOTSTRAP.md pending across restart before first onboarding`
- User-content stale repair: existing `memory/` / `skills/` completion tests

What failed before this fix, if known?

Preseeded profile files triggered bootstrap completion and deleted `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first user onboarding.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Yes — preseeded managed workspaces keep `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real completion evidence.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

No schema/config migration.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Profile-only completion after restart when `bootstrapSeededAt` is already persisted.

How is that risk mitigated?

While `BOOTSTRAP.md` exists and `setupCompletedAt` is unset, completion evidence requires durable user-content (`memory/`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`); profile diffs never suffice across restarts.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review — no contributor-facing blockers (`status: 👀 ready for maintainer look`).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer picks canonical branch: this PR (#91988, 🐚 platinum hermit) vs #91955 (🦐 gold shrimp, waiting on author)
- Maintainer accepts intentional compatibility/session-state boundary (`merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state`)

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- All ClawSweeper P1/P2 code findings through `4ee84a9370`
- Labels now: `rating: 🐚 platinum hermit`, `proof: supplied`, `status: 👀 ready for maintainer look`, `size: S`
- CI: 0 failing / 0 pending on head `4ee84a9370`

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false-positive group allowlist warning for parent-only channel records (#92684)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix false-positive doctor warning: when a channel has per-account configs with
populated `groupAllowFrom` lists, the empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` no longer
triggers a misleading "all group messages will be silently dropped" warning.

## Root Cause

`scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` called `checkAccount` on the top-level
channel record as if it were a standalone account — even when active per-account
sub-records existed. When the top-level `groupAllowFrom` was empty (serving only
as a fallback), the doctor treated it as an unconfigured account and emitted the
group-drop warning. In reality, every account supplied its own populated
`groupAllowFrom`, so the top-level empty list was never read by the runtime.

Two code defects were present in the initial fix:

1. **TS2345 type error**: `asObjectRecord()` returns `Record<string, unknown> | null`,
   so `accounts && Object.values(...).some(...)` yields `boolean | null` rather than
   `boolean`. Fixed with `Boolean()` coercion.
2. **Plugin extra-warning path not gated**: `suppressGroupWarning` only wrapped the
   shared default warning hook via `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning`,
   but Telegram channels skip the default hook and emit group-allowlist warnings
   through `extraWarningsForAccount` instead. The parent-scope extra-warning call
   was not gated, leaving the Telegram false-positive path reachable. Fixed by
   gating `extraWarningsForAccount` under `!suppressGroupWarning`.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
Doctor no longer emits a false-positive group-allowlist warning for an empty
top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every per-account record carries its own
populated allowlist. The plugin extra-warning path (Telegram) is also gated.
Non-regression: DM allowlist warnings still fire on parent records; group
warnings still fire when no per-account configs exist.

### environment
- OS: Windows 10 Enterprise LTSC 2019
- Runtime: Node.js 22.x, tsx (direct TypeScript execution, no vitest, no mocks)
- Setup: openclaw workspace at fix commit (52ae4a23), production source modules

### steps
1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level
   `groupAllowFrom`, and accounts each with populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Import `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` directly from production source via tsx.
3. Call the scanner with the config; observe 0 warnings.
4. Verify non-regression: parent-only channel still warns; DM allowlist warning
   still fires on parent records.

### observedResult

**Direct scanner invocation via tsx (production source, no vitest):**
```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92684-proof.mjs

[1/4] importing scanner...
[2/4] scanner imported
[3/4] Scenario 1: parent + populated per-account allowlists
  done in 4799ms, 0 warning(s) (expected: 0)
[4/4] Scenario 2: parent-only, no accounts
  1 warning(s) (expected: 1)
    - channels.telegram.groupPolicy is "allowlist" but groupAllowFrom
      (and allowFrom) is empty — all group messages will be silently
      dropped. Add sender IDs to channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom or
      channels.telegram.allowFrom, or set groupPolicy to "open".
       Scenario 3: DM allowlist still fires on parent
  1 warning(s) (expected: 1, DM only)
    - channels.telegram.dmPolicy is "allowlist" but allowFrom is
      empty — all DMs will be blocked. Add sender IDs to
      channels.telegram.allowFrom, or run "openclaw doctor --fix"
      to auto-migrate from pairing store when entries exist.

============================================================
  RESULTS
============================================================
  Scenario 1 (parent + accounts): PASS (got 0, expected 0)
  Scenario 2 (parent-only):       PASS (got 1, expected 1)
  Scenario 3 (DM preserved):      PASS (got 1, expected 1 DM)
  Overall: ALL PASS
============================================================
```

**Test suite (vitest):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

All 16 existing doctor tests pass; no regressions.

**Production change (`src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts`):**
```diff
-    checkAccount(channelConfig, `channels.${channelName}`, channelName);
-
     const accounts = asObjectRecord(channelConfig.accounts);
+    const hasActiveAccounts = Boolean(
+      accounts &&
+        Object.values(accounts).some((a) => !isDisabledRecord(a)),
+    );
+
+    // When the channel has active per-account configs, the top-level record
+    // serves only as a parent fallback. Suppress the default
+    // empty-group-allowlist warning so an empty top-level groupAllowFrom
+    // does not produce a false positive when every account supplies its own
+    // populated allowlist (issue #92684).
+    checkAccount(
+      channelConfig,
+      `channels.${channelName}`,
+      channelName,
+      undefined,
+      hasActiveAccounts,
+    );
```

In `checkAccount`:
```diff
-    if (params.extraWarningsForAccount) {
+    if (!suppressGroupWarning && params.extraWarningsForAccount) {
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 3 passed
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts` — 13 passed
- [x] `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92684-proof.mjs` — 3/3 scenarios pass (direct scanner invocation)
- [x] Change is minimal (single file, 2 logical fixes)

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

target.title: fix(launcher): detect Bun's module-not-found errors in `openclaw.mjs` (#86198)
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw` fails to start under Bun with:

```
error: Cannot find module './dist/warning-filter.js' from '.../openclaw.mjs'
```

The launcher in `openclaw.mjs` intentionally treats the optional `./dist/warning-filter.{js,mjs}` import as best-effort and silently continues if absent. The guard `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` checks `err.code === 'ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND'`, but Bun (v1.3.x) does not set that code on its module-resolution errors, so the error is re-thrown and the process exits.

There is a textual fallback already, but it only matches Node's absolute-path message form (`fileURLToPath()` against `import.meta.url`). Bun's error message contains the raw relative specifier instead.

## Fix

1. `isModuleNotFoundError` now also returns `true` when the error message matches `/Cannot find module/`, covering Bun.
2. `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` now additionally matches Bun's message form, which embeds the original specifier (e.g. `'./dist/warning-filter.js'`) rather than the resolved absolute path.

Strict superset of prior behavior on Node — same launcher continues to skip missing optional imports and rethrow real failures.

## Verification

Simulated Bun-style error against the relaxed message check:

```
$ node -e '...' # uses raw Bun-format error string
bun-msg matches: true
```

Closes #86198

target.title: fix(imessage): remove client-side split-send coalescing (imsg coalesces upstream)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #91243.

imsg `>= 0.11.1` coalesces Apple's URL-preview split-sends upstream (openclaw/imsg#141, shipped in v0.11.1), so OpenClaw now receives a single logical message for `Dump https://example.com` and no longer needs to debounce-and-merge the split rows client-side. This removes the entire `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms` opt-in path and the dead `balloon_bundle_id` plumbing it depended on.

This is the planned teardown of #90858 (the interim client-side implementation).

## What changed

**Config removal + migration**
- Dropped `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms` from `types.imessage.ts` and the zod schema; regenerated `bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` and the doc baseline.
- Added a `doctor --fix` migration in the imessage doctor contract (`legacyConfigRules` + `normalizeCompatibilityConfig`) that drops the stale key at the imessage root **and** per account — mirroring the existing retired-`catchup` precedent. No runtime compat shim.

**Monitor**
- Removed the 2.5 s `debounceMsOverride`, the `chat:sender` DM bucketing, the `shouldDebounce` DM opt-in branch, the `shouldCombineIMessagePayloadBucket` balloon-metadata flush gate, and the session `imsgEmitsBalloonMetadata` latch. DMs no longer carry per-message latency.
- Deleted `IMESSAGE_URL_BALLOON_BUNDLE_ID`, `hasIMessageUrlBalloonBundleID`, `hasIMessageBalloonMetadata`, and the now-unconsumed `balloon_bundle_id` payload field + parser validation.

**Preserved (deliberate)**
- The general same-sender inbound debounce (`messages.inbound`, off by default) is kept: `combineIMessagePayloads` still merges a multi-entry bucket into one agent turn, exactly as Discord/Telegram/Slack do. This is a documented cross-channel feature (`docs/concepts/messages.md` § Inbound debouncing), not split-send-specific. Removing it would make iMessage the odd channel out and contradict the docs.

**Docs**
- Rewrote the iMessage "Split-send DMs" section to explain that imsg handles coalescing upstream (no config needed, no added latency).
- Updated the BlueBubbles migration tables and the `messages.md` debounce note.

## Acceptance criteria (#91243)

- [x] `coalesceSameSenderDms` removed from the config surface; `doctor --fix` migrates existing configs (root + per-account).
- [x] iMessage monitor no longer debounces DMs for split-send merging (no per-DM latency).
- [x] URL split-sends still arrive as a single agent turn (now via imsg `>= 0.11.1`).
- [x] Net non-test LOC decreases (**−68**: +72 / −140 excluding tests, generated, docs).

## Scope note

This does **not** add a hard imsg-version gate in probe code. The issue treats "imsg `>= 0.11.1` is the supported floor" as a maintainer declaration; enforcing a runtime version check is out of scope and risks breaking working setups. The docs state the `>= 0.11.1` requirement for split-send coalescing.

## Tests

- `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts`, `parse-notification.test.ts`, `monitor.last-route.test.ts`, `doctor-contract-api.test.ts` — updated; added coalesce-migration coverage and a general-debounce merge test. The recovery-cursor tests now flush rows individually (modeling the real non-debounced path).
- `src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts` — green.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test`, `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:core:test` — green.
- `config:channels:check`, oxfmt, docs format — green.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(feishu): route SDK HTTP through ambient proxy
target.body:
AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

- Route Feishu/Lark SDK HTTP requests through OpenClaw's ambient proxy agent and set `proxy: false` so axios does not take a separate HTTPS proxy path.
- Reuse the same proxy-aware HTTP instance for `WSClient` bootstrap requests while preserving WebSocket heartbeat config and callbacks.
- Preserve request-level `httpAgent`/`httpsAgent` values for ordinary ambient proxy env, but override them while OpenClaw managed proxy mode is active so Feishu SDK HTTP cannot bypass the operator proxy boundary.

Fixes #48949

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/extension-shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/feishu/src/client.ts extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test:extension feishu`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (twice before the managed-proxy follow-up; no actionable regressions found)

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu token/API and WebSocket bootstrap failed when the gateway process had `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` configured, producing `tenant_access_token` failures and 400 responses containing "The plain HTTP request was sent to HTTPS port".

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu host running OpenClaw 2026.5.22 with the systemd user gateway and `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY`/`OPENCLAW_PROXY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890`. To avoid replacing this source checkout into my live gateway, I first applied the equivalent dist hotfix to the installed Feishu plugin, restarted the gateway, then ported the same fix to source in this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Restarted the installed OpenClaw gateway with the equivalent Feishu dist hotfix, then ran `systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway`, `openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe`, and a filtered `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` check for both prior failure strings and successful Feishu startup lines.

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output and runtime log excerpt from the live OpenClaw gateway after the fix:

```console
$ systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway
active

$ openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe
Checking channel status (probe)...
Gateway reachable.
- Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works

Tip: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli#status adds gateway health probes to status output (requires a reachable gateway).

$ journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since '2026-05-25 15:12:04' --no-pager | rg 'Request failed with status code 400|connect failed|unable to connect|socket hang up|tenant_access_token|bot identity|ws client ready|bot open_id resolved'
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.501+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: bot open_id resolved: ou_[redacted]
May 25 15:12:04 jamietop node[13765]: 2026-05-25T15:12:04.945+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
```

The probe reported `Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works`; gateway logs showed `bot open_id resolved` and `[ws] ws client ready`. The log grep included the prior failure strings (`Request failed with status code 400`, `connect failed`, `unable to connect`, `socket hang up`, and `tenant_access_token`) and returned only the two successful startup lines after the final restart.

Observed result after fix: Feishu API probing and WebSocket startup both recovered in the proxy-enabled environment.

What was not tested: I did not swap this source checkout into the live gateway or run the repository-wide `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR includes targeted source tests, `test:extension feishu`, `check:changed`, managed-proxy agent override unit coverage, and live validation through the equivalent installed-package hotfix.

target.title: Feishu channel crashes after upgrading to 2026.5.2 — appId/appSecret fields incompatible
target.body:
## Summary

- Bundled channel config metadata generated by `scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts` marks Zod `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as JSON Schema `required`, because Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` treats "has a default value" as "always present at runtime".
- This produced 35 defaulted-required leaks across 17 channels in `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`; on Feishu alone, eight defaulted fields (`domain`, `connectionMode`, `webhookPath`, `dmPolicy`, `groupPolicy`, `reactionNotifications`, `typingIndicator`, `resolveSenderNames`) were listed as `required`, so the bundled schema rejected the very config shapes that the runtime Zod parser accepted.
- The reporter's #77116 config (omitting `domain`, `connectionMode`, `webhookPath`, `reactionNotifications`, `typingIndicator`, `resolveSenderNames`) was rejected by JSON Schema validation while the runtime Zod parser accepted it, contributing to the channel-config crash loop after the 2026.5.x upgrade.

Why now: the Codex review on #77116 confirmed that current `main` still has the generator-level bug, and tagged the issue `P1`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`, and `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`. A source-level generator repair is the narrow, root-cause-shaped next step.

Intended outcome: bundled channel config metadata stops marking defaulted fields as `required`, while the runtime Zod parser continues to supply the same defaults. The packaged gateway's JSON Schema validation accepts the same shapes the runtime parser accepts.

Out of scope: the reporter's separately installed `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` external plugin path is not touched. Stale external plugin metadata / doctor cleanup is left for maintainer follow-up, as flagged by the Codex review.

Success looks like: `node scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write` regenerates bundled metadata with zero `required` fields across all 24 channels; the reporter's config from #77116 is accepted by both the runtime Zod parser and the generated JSON Schema, with and without `applyDefaults`.

Reviewers should focus on: the `relaxDefaultedRequired` helper added to `src/channels/plugins/config-schema.ts`, the regenerated `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`, and the four new regression cases under `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts > FeishuConfigSchema #77116 regression`.

## Linked context

Closes #77116

Related: Codex review on #77116 (referenced commit `a189baa4b344`), Feishu channel schema history at `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts`.

Not requested by a maintainer; this is an external contribution targeting the source-level generator bug identified in the Codex review.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `channels.feishu` config (and the same shape on 16 other channels) was rejected by bundled JSON Schema validation because Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` marked `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as `required`, while the runtime Zod parser accepted the same shapes. This mismatch contributed to the gateway crash loop in #77116.
- Real environment tested: source checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on `main`, with the patch applied and bundled channel config metadata regenerated in-place via `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Regenerate bundled metadata: `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write`
  - Inspect Feishu schema's `required` and `default` keywords in the regenerated file (script-driven check below).
  - Run the regression test set covering the generator, the Feishu runtime parser, the generated JSON Schema (with and without defaults), the bundled runtime test, and the shared schema validator / defaults helpers.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output): Bundled metadata regeneration writes the new file:

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write
[bundled-channel-config-metadata] wrote src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts
```

Bundled metadata no longer marks any Feishu property as required, while every default value is preserved:

```
=== Feishu #77116 bundled schema regression summary ===
schema.required (current/main after fix): []
defaulted property count: 8
  domain                 default="feishu"         ok (default supplies value)
  connectionMode         default="websocket"      ok (default supplies value)
  webhookPath            default="/feishu/events" ok (default supplies value)
  dmPolicy               default="pairing"        ok (default supplies value)
  groupPolicy            default="allowlist"      ok (default supplies value)
  reactionNotifications  default="own"            ok (default supplies value)
  typingIndicator        default=true             ok (default supplies value)
  resolveSenderNames     default=true             ok (default supplies value)

PASS: 0 defaulted fields leaked into required.
```

Regression test set covering generator + runtime + JSON Schema acceptance:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts \
    src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts \
    src/config/bundled-channel-config-runtime.test.ts \
    src/plugins/schema-validator.test.ts \
    src/shared/json-schema-defaults.test.ts

 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  85 passed (85)
```

Exit code: 0

- Observed result after fix: validation passes with zero `required` fields across all 24 bundled channels (previously 35 defaulted-required leaks across 17 channels); the reporter's #77116 config is accepted by both the runtime Zod parser and the generated JSON Schema, with and without `applyDefaults: true`.
- What was not tested: a live packaged upgrade from 2026.4.x to a build containing this patch was not run in this environment (no npm packaged build available locally); runtime telemetry / live gateway crash-loop instrumentation was not added.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: validation is source-level (Vitest on the worktree) and bundled-metadata regeneration against the same source tree. The reporter's environment additionally used the external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` plugin, which is intentionally out of scope; the bundled schema path that this PR repairs is the path the Codex review identified as still broken on `main`.
- Before evidence: the previously generated `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` listed `schema.required` as `["domain","connectionMode","webhookPath","dmPolicy","groupPolicy","reactionNotifications","typingIndicator","resolveSenderNames"]` for Feishu, while every one of those eight fields carried a `default` keyword in `properties`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write` (regenerate bundled metadata after the generator fix).
- `pnpm config set verify-deps-before-run false` (workaround so `pnpm exec` does not retrigger install in this worktree; not part of the patch).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/schema-validator.test.ts src/shared/json-schema-defaults.test.ts` — 85 / 85 passing.

Regression coverage added:

- `src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts`:
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema > does not mark fields with defaults as required (Feishu #77116 regression)` — asserts that `.default(...)` and `.optional().default(...)` fields are dropped from `required` while non-defaulted fields stay required.
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema > keeps defaulted fields reachable as properties and as runtime defaults` — asserts that properties still carry `default` and `safeParse({})` still returns the default value.
- `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts > FeishuConfigSchema #77116 regression (appId/appSecret/defaulted-required)` — four cases:
  - reporter config from #77116 is accepted by `FeishuConfigSchema.safeParse`,
  - `buildChannelConfigSchema(FeishuConfigSchema).schema.required` is `[]`,
  - reporter config is accepted by `validateJsonSchemaValue` with `applyDefaults: true`,
  - reporter config is accepted by `validateJsonSchemaValue` with `applyDefaults: false`.

Extension-specific plugin contract test: the Feishu extension test suite runs the shared schema generator against the extension's Zod schema, and asserts the generator output matches the runtime parser contract for the #77116 reporter config (valid extension id, valid account schemas, valid default application paths). This is the extension test that catches any future regression of the bundled-schema acceptance for Feishu.

What failed before this fix: the new `does not mark fields with defaults as required (Feishu #77116 regression)` test failed before implementation with `expected [ 'alwaysRequired', 'withDefault', 'optionalWithDefault' ] to deeply equal [ 'alwaysRequired' ]`, confirming the bug shape before the fix was applied.

The pre-existing `buildChannelConfigSchema > builds json schema when toJSONSchema is available` assertion was updated to drop `required: ["enabled"]`, because that test enshrined the exact behavior this PR fixes.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No` for end users; `Yes` for tools that consume the bundled channel config metadata as JSON Schema (they now see fewer `required` constraints, matching the runtime parser contract).

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — bundled JSON Schema is regenerated; `required` arrays shrink or disappear for channels that previously listed defaulted fields. No config migration is required from end users: any config the runtime parser already accepts is now also accepted by the bundled JSON Schema.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: external tooling (IDE extensions, doctor checks, packaging tools) that relied on the bundled JSON Schema's `required` arrays to mean "must be present in user config". Such tooling will now correctly treat defaulted fields as optional, which matches the runtime Zod parser's behavior; no field that lacked a default loses its `required` marker.

How is that risk mitigated? The helper is recursive and only drops a property from `required` when that same property carries a `default` keyword in the same schema object, so non-defaulted required fields stay required everywhere. Regression coverage includes the `alwaysRequired` non-defaulted field staying in `required`, plus the runtime default-application behavior is preserved by the unchanged `safeParse` path.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Await maintainer review on the generator-level scope (one shared helper, regenerated bundled metadata, no channel-specific shims).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on the author side — local validation, bundled-metadata regeneration, and regression coverage are all in place. Awaiting maintainer review and CI verification once the PR opens.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? The Codex review on #77116 explicitly identified the generator-level input-schema repair as the next step; this PR implements that repair and regenerates the bundled metadata in the same commit.

- Fix classification: root-cause generator-level fix (shared schema helper, no channel-specific shims). The fix targets the `buildChannelConfigSchema` helper that generates JSON Schema from the Zod parser for every bundled channel; the upstream root is in the schema normalization layer, not in any individual channel's runtime behavior.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high. The fix is local, recursive, fully covered by regression tests, and the regenerated bundled metadata is verifiable by anyone running the generator script.
- Root cause: Zod v4's `toJSONSchema` marks `.optional().default(...)` and `.default(...)` fields as JSON Schema `required`, because Zod treats "has a default value" as "always present at runtime". The bundled metadata generator passed that output straight through, so 35 defaulted fields across 17 channels (8 on Feishu) leaked into `required` while the runtime Zod parser still applied the same defaults. This is a generator-level schema contract mismatch, not a per-channel bug.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the repair lands in the shared `buildChannelConfigSchema` helper used by every bundled channel, so all 24 channels benefit from one change instead of needing per-channel shims. The bundled schema now matches the runtime parser contract because the source of the divergence (Zod `toJSONSchema` semantics) is post-processed by the helper, and the regenerated metadata is checked in alongside the helper so the contract stays consistent.
- What did NOT change: per-channel schemas (`extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts` and the other 23 channels' Zod schemas) are unchanged — only the shared generator and its regenerated output change. No migration, no config breaking change, no public API change; the only behavior change is that the bundled JSON Schema stops rejecting config shapes the runtime parser already accepts. Out of scope: the external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` plugin path, doctor cleanup, and any non-bundled-schema packaging follow-up.

target.title: fix(codex): honor bound agent exec host policy
target.body:
## Summary
- store the binding agent id for newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings
- pass the stored binding agent id into the Codex native execution guard
- block bound app-server turns when that stored agent resolves to `tools.exec.host=node`, even without a session key

## Scope
This intentionally applies to newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings. Existing saved bindings that predate `data.agentId` can keep the previous fallback behavior until the conversation is detached and rebound.

That keeps this PR narrow: it does not add migration or legacy binding inference logic.

## Verification
